title: Consume Ratconfig UI revision c6ee666
description:
## Why

Yazelix still packages Ratconfig revision `5883db3`, so the config popup does not contain the published Unicode-width correction, light-mode contrast fixes, darker inactive tabs and pane headings, or simplified pane chrome through `c6ee666`. Yazelix owns the pinned child revision and product-level Mars dogfooding.

## Evidence

- `flake.nix:50-53` declares Ratconfig as a non-flake GitHub input.
- `flake.lock:281-295` locks Ratconfig to `5883db3a40f81dc886a7f557d8c7b2c33097147e`.
- Published Ratconfig commits after that pin are `e755d9f` (Unicode field-column widths), `dc0e8bb` (light-mode contrast), and `c6ee666` (light-mode tabs/titles and pane-border polish); `95539c2` is the intervening child-ledger closure.
- Closed Yazelix bead `yazelix-5vl-mq4` records the ownership split: Ratconfig owns renderer semantics; Yazelix owns the dependency pin and product integration.

## User-Visible Contract

The packaged config popup consumes Ratconfig `c6ee666`: light appearance keeps semantic text readable, inactive tabs and the `settings`/`details` headings render black, selected semantic colors remain intact, Unicode setting labels retain correct terminal-cell alignment, and the two body panes use flush outer rails plus one shared vertical divider without top/bottom pane rails. Dark appearance remains readable and otherwise unchanged.

## Scope

In scope:
- Update only the Ratconfig input lock to exact revision `c6ee6662013afd2d832c391d206d63ff1f6c8c1f` while preserving concurrent lockfile work.
- Run the Yazelix flake, package, temporary-profile, and config-popup integration checks against the remote child revision with no local override.
- Dogfood the normal transparent popup in Mars light and dark appearance, including tabs, pane headings, selected/error rows, structured details, Unicode labels, narrow width, outer rails, and the shared divider.
- Explicitly judge whether the tab row remains sufficiently separated from the borderless pane headings. If it is ambiguous, capture the runtime view and return to the maintainer before choosing any separator treatment.
- Update `CHANGELOG.md` for the visible popup behavior and refresh the README LOC scorecard as required by repository policy.
- Refresh the installed Yazelix profile after the verified runtime-flake change.

Out of scope:
- Do not edit Ratconfig source, copy its palette/layout into Yazelix, vendor it, or use a committed local-path override.
- Do not change Mars colors, transparency, the 50/50 pane split, config schema, validation, file IO, or apply behavior.
- Do not invent a replacement horizontal separator without maintainer approval after dogfooding.
- Do not update unrelated flake inputs.

## Implementation Notes

1. Record the pre-existing dirty `flake.lock` diff, then update only the Ratconfig input and verify the resulting locked revision exactly.
2. Inspect the lock diff before running checks; retain every unrelated user-owned change.
3. Run automated integration from the remote lock, then install through a temporary profile.
4. Dogfood both appearances. Treat the missing tab/body divider as an explicit visual experiment, not an already-settled design contract.
5. Update current-state documentation and the LOC scorecard without migration-style prose.
6. Upgrade the user profile only after all package and manual checks pass.

## Verification

- `jq -r '.nodes.ratconfig.locked.rev' flake.lock` prints `c6ee6662013afd2d832c391d206d63ff1f6c8c1f`.
- `nix flake check` exits 0.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` exits 0.
- `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs` exits 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix --profile <tmp>` exits 0 and the temporary install uses the remote locked child.
- Manual Mars light-mode dogfooding confirms tab/title contrast, semantic selected-row contrast, Unicode alignment, and the simplified three-rail pane geometry; dark-mode dogfooding finds no regression.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix` succeeds after acceptance.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if updating Ratconfig would overwrite or entangle unrelated in-progress `flake.lock` changes; isolate the single input update first.
- Stop if the lock resolves to a different Ratconfig revision; inspect the child range rather than silently consuming extra commits.
- Stop and return with a screenshot if removing the horizontal pane rails makes the tab-to-body boundary unclear; do not choose a replacement separator autonomously.
- Stop if integration requires Yazelix-owned copies of Ratconfig styles/layout or a Mars palette change.
- Stop if any automated gate fails twice for the same reason after a reasonable focused fix.

## Maintenance Notes

- Dependency decision: update the existing pinned Ratconfig input only; add no package, crate, adapter, or second style owner.
- Effort S. Risk LOW/MED because automated ownership is narrow but final acceptance depends on transparent runtime rendering. Confidence HIGH.
- Child reference: `ratconfig-light-mode-contrast-nh8`; published revision: `c6ee6662013afd2d832c391d206d63ff1f6c8c1f`.
- Rejected alternatives: vendoring Ratconfig, a local-path production override, duplicating theme roles in Yazelix, weakening the selected-row contract, and preemptively restoring a horizontal rail before runtime review.
notes: Implementation staged against exact Ratconfig revision c6ee6662013afd2d832c391d206d63ff1f6c8c1f. Checked remote tip first: origin/main is b9844f2 and contains c6ee666, but also introduces the separately gated Core/All public API while ratconfig-core-all-settings-5d9.3 remains open, so tip adoption was rejected for this visual-only bead. The lock diff changes only Ratconfig from 5883db3 to c6ee666. Added current-state CHANGELOG wording and corrected the reproducible scorecard to 18,217 lines; the prior Rust count was stale by two lines after rustfmt. Verification passed: exact revision assertion, git diff check, nix flake check with all 40 yzx-config tests, nix flake show --all-systems, nix build .#yazelix, temporary-profile install/status, and installed runtime refresh. Manual light/dark Mars dogfooding remains before closure.
close_reason: Completed: pinned Ratconfig c6ee666, all automated integration gates passed, and maintainer dogfooding confirmed the light/dark contrast, Unicode alignment, and simplified pane geometry.

title: Keep Zellij legible with Mars light appearance
description:
## Why

Mars light appearance updates the terminal and Ratconfig, but an inherited Zellij theme still resolves to Zellij's dark-oriented `default` styling. In a managed session this makes tabs, pane frames, status text, and other Zellij chrome difficult to read. The user currently has to discover the separate Zellij theme picker after the contrast has already degraded.

## Evidence

- Runtime dogfooding on 2026-07-17: switching Mars to light mode while Zellij remains on `default` produces very poor Zellij contrast.
- `crates/yzx-config/src/model.rs:184-197` maps `mars.appearance.preset` only to Ratconfig's light/dark renderer theme; it does not resolve Zellij appearance.
- `crates/yzx-config/src/zellij_sidecar.rs:13-30` models the inherited Zellij theme as `default` and exposes the fork's packaged choices.
- `crates/yzx-config/src/zellij_sidecar.rs:32-64` already owns sparse theme writes and live watched-config refreshes.
- `../yazelix-zellij/zellij-utils/src/data.rs:1439-1470,1559-1569` defines the native default styling from fixed eight-bit colors and classifies it as dark.
- `crates/yzx-config/zellij-themes.txt` includes several packaged light themes, while closed bead `yazelix-5vl-core-all-config-discovery-lsw.12` deliberately deferred cross-application theme synchronization.

## User-visible contract

When `mars.appearance.preset` is `light` and the Zellij theme is inherited/default, `yzx config` must not leave the user unaware of the resulting contrast problem.

Prefer the smallest behavior that keeps Zellij legible automatically both in the active managed session and on the next launch, but only if it remains derived behavior and does not materialize a user override. If reliable automatic adaptation requires another watcher, duplicated theme state, or implicit persistence, show a non-blocking contextual Ratconfig tip instead. The tip must explain that Zellij remains on its dark-oriented default and route the user clearly to the existing Zellij theme picker.

An explicitly selected packaged or custom Zellij theme is user intent and must never be overwritten by a Mars appearance change. Returning Mars to dark may restore inherited dark behavior only when the Zellij theme is still inherited.

## Ownership

- Yazelix `yzx-config` owns the cross-tool product policy, state resolution, and any contextual diagnostic.
- Mars remains the sole owner of terminal appearance.
- Zellij and the pinned fork remain the owners of theme palettes and theme application.
- Ratconfig remains generic: it may render an existing host-declared non-blocking diagnostic, but must not learn Mars or Zellij policy.

## Scope

In scope:
- Reproduce the failing Zellij roles with Mars light plus inherited `default` theme.
- Compare three bounded fixes in order: a genuinely appearance-compatible inherited Zellij default, a derived dark/light Zellij selection for inherited state, and a conditional Ratconfig tip.
- Test candidate packaged light themes or the native `ansi` theme under Mars light and dark rather than assuming their names imply adequate contrast.
- Preserve sparse Zellij intent, custom theme names, live watched-config updates, resets, Home Manager ownership, and next-session behavior.
- Keep the result compatible with the sparse-intent Overview migration in `yazelix-5vl-core-all-config-discovery-lsw.13`.
- Update current-state docs, CHANGELOG, and README LOC scorecard if runtime or UI behavior changes.

Out of scope:
- A general Mars/Zellij/Helix/Yazi theme synchronizer, user-defined theme-pair registry, theme preview system, contrast classifier, or new setting.
- Writing a Zellij sidecar override merely because Mars changed.
- Product-specific logic in Ratconfig, copied Zellij palettes in Yazelix, or changing the Zellij fork's themes without a separately proven generic defect.
- Warning about every explicit theme based only on its name.

## Implementation Notes

1. Establish a state matrix for Mars dark/light against Zellij inherited/default, explicit packaged light/dark, custom, and externally managed values.
2. First test whether one native inherited behavior can stay legible in both Mars appearances without adding state. Reject it if dogfooding shows compromised contrast in either mode.
3. If automatic derived selection is still considered, keep it in one Yazelix owner, apply it live and at session materialization, and ensure reset/explicit intent semantics remain truthful. Do not persist the derived choice to `zellij/config.kdl`.
4. If that path introduces another state machine or watcher, use the existing host diagnostic/details surface to attach one non-blocking, actionable tip to the Mars appearance or Zellij theme field. The tip should disappear when Mars is dark or Zellij has explicit intent.
5. Add one focused behavioral contract for the selected path plus preservation checks for explicit and custom themes.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzx-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 and prove light/default handling plus explicit-theme preservation.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` should exit 0.
- `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix --profile <tmp>` should install successfully.
- Manual dogfooding should cover a fresh light session, a live dark-to-light change with inherited Zellij theme, an explicit packaged/custom theme, and the return to dark. Zellij tabs, frames, and status text must remain readable or the contextual tip must be immediately visible and actionable.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop automatic synchronization if it writes or erases explicit Zellij intent, needs polling/watchers, or creates a second theme-state owner.
- Stop before changing Ratconfig if its existing host-declared diagnostic/details model can express the tip.
- Stop before modifying Mars or the Zellij fork unless a generic owner-level defect is independently demonstrated.
- Stop if a supposedly adaptive default or chosen light theme is not legible in manual transparent Mars dogfooding.

## Maintenance Notes

Effort: S for a contextual tip, M for a clean inherited adaptive path. Risk: LOW/MED. Confidence: HIGH in the contrast defect and existing picker; MED in automatic adaptation without extra state.

Dependency decision: reuse the current Rust/std model, Zellij theme inventory, sidecar writer, and Ratconfig diagnostic surface. Add no crate or package.

Rejected alternatives: silently persisting a light Zellij theme, overwriting explicit custom themes, teaching Ratconfig cross-tool policy, copying palettes into Yazelix, and building general theme synchronization for one confirmed contrast boundary.

title: Audit and retire stale Yazelix worktrees
description:
## Why

Yazelix currently registers three secondary worktrees in addition to the primary checkout. They include one known disposable repin worktree, one worktree whose `.git` file points at a moved/nonexistent administrative directory, and one clean but historically divergent audit branch. Blind deletion could discard untracked files or branch-only work, while leaving all of them indefinitely creates workspace and branch ambiguity.

## Evidence

- `git worktree list --porcelain` on 2026-07-16 listed the primary checkout plus `yazelix_light_mode`, `yazelix_rust_loc_audit`, and detached `yazelix-mars-repin-20260716`.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix` is the primary `main` checkout at `79a18c0a`, two commits behind `origin/main`, with unrelated tracked modifications. It must not be deleted or reset as part of this cleanup.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-mars-repin-20260716` is detached at `dc197a1a`, clean, fully contained in `origin/main`, and was created only for the Mars repin transaction.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/worktrees/yazelix_light_mode/.git` points to nonexistent `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix/.git/worktrees/yazelix_light_mode`, so ordinary `git -C ... status` fails. Its branch tip is `67371615` (`feat: add yzxterm appearance handling`), it has no remote branch, and it has no merge base with current `origin/main`.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/worktrees/yazelix_rust_loc_audit` is clean at `b05ea629` (`Refresh Rust LOC ownership audit`), has no remote branch, and has no merge base with current `origin/main`.

## Scope

In scope:
- Inventory every registered Yazelix worktree, its administrative metadata, status, branch/ref, remote publication, reachability, and unique work.
- Treat all secondary worktrees as removal candidates, but preserve any dirty, untracked, or still-useful branch-only work before removal.
- Repair the stale `yazelix_light_mode` worktree pointer temporarily if needed to obtain a trustworthy status, or make a filesystem-level backup before any manual cleanup.
- Decide whether the two old branch tips contain anything worth porting to current Nova; preserve selected work as explicit beads/commits rather than keeping ambiguous worktrees indefinitely.
- Remove safe secondary worktrees with `git worktree remove`, then prune stale administrative entries and optionally delete obsolete local branches only after their tips are preserved or explicitly rejected.
- Leave the primary checkout and its current uncommitted changes intact; after secondary cleanup, report how its local `main` should be advanced safely.

Out of scope:
- Do not reset, clean, stash, commit, or delete files from the primary dirty checkout without separate maintainer approval.
- Do not force-delete an uninspectable or unmerged worktree merely to reduce the count.
- Do not port light-mode or Rust-audit code in this bead; create focused follow-ups for anything deliberately retained.
- Do not delete remote branches or rewrite repository history.

## Implementation Notes

1. Fetch `origin/main` and capture `git worktree list --porcelain`, `git branch -vv`, and `git worktree prune --dry-run --verbose`.
2. For every secondary path, record `git status --porcelain=v1`, HEAD, branch, upstream, remote branch existence, and reachability from `origin/main`.
3. For `yazelix_light_mode`, first preserve the directory, then use `git worktree repair` or the main repository metadata to restore inspection. If status still cannot be trusted, stop before deletion and present the backup/recovery choice.
4. For branch-only histories with no merge base, inspect the tip tree and a bounded set of relevant commits rather than assuming thousands of historical commits are intentional unique work.
5. Remove only worktrees classified as clean and disposable, using normal `git worktree remove`; use `--force` only with explicit maintainer approval after preservation evidence.
6. Run `git worktree prune`, verify the final list, and record retained branches/worktrees and their reasons.

## Verification

- `git worktree list --porcelain` should show only the primary checkout plus any explicitly retained worktrees.
- `git worktree prune --dry-run --verbose` should report no stale administrative entries after cleanup.
- `git status --porcelain=v1` in the primary checkout should show the same pre-existing user changes; cleanup must not alter them.
- Every removed branch tip must either be reachable from a retained ref, recorded in an explicit backup ref, or documented as intentionally discarded with maintainer approval.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop before deletion if a worktree is dirty, contains untracked files, cannot be inspected reliably, or has useful work not preserved elsewhere.
- Stop before deleting any local branch whose tip is not reachable from a retained ref unless the maintainer explicitly rejects that work.
- Stop if repairing worktree metadata would overwrite the primary checkout metadata or working files.

## Maintenance Notes

- Effort: S-M. Risk: MED because two old branches are unrelated to current Nova history. Confidence: HIGH that the temporary repin worktree is disposable; LOW until inspection for the stale light-mode directory; MED for retiring the Rust LOC audit worktree.
- Rejected alternative: delete every secondary directory immediately. Worktree count is less important than preserving unmerged or untracked work.
- Prefer a final state with one authoritative primary checkout and short-lived task worktrees that are removed after their commits are published.
close_reason: Removed all three secondary worktrees: detached Mars repin, clean Rust LOC audit, and repaired/forced stale light-mode. Preserved local branch refs yazelix_light_mode at 67371615 and yazelix_rust_loc_audit at b05ea629, so committed history remains recoverable. Explicit maintainer stale designation authorized discarding the light-mode worktree's sole uncommitted change: the obsolete in-progress Restore Linux Foot terminal variant planning line. Primary dirty checkout was not reset or otherwise altered. Final worktree list contains only the primary checkout and prune dry-run is empty.

title: Document macOS XDG opt-in and declarative config source reuse
description:
## Why

A macOS Home Manager or nix-darwin user may prefer Linux-style XDG locations and one declarative source for configuration used both inside and outside Yazelix. Nova intentionally keeps its managed runtime under a Yazelix-owned namespaced tree and does not adopt ambient Helix, Yazi, Zellij, terminal, Starship, or shell files automatically. The supported answer needs concise documentation rather than another runtime integration mode.

## Outcome

Document two independent opt-in patterns:

1. How macOS users can deliberately export Linux-style XDG base directories through their own Home Manager or nix-darwin launchd configuration so GUI-launched and shell-launched tools see a consistent environment.
2. How Home Manager users can reuse the same source files for a normal host tool and the corresponding programs.yazelix.config native text/source option while installing them at separate owned destinations.

State clearly that XDG_CONFIG_HOME changes the base directory but Yazelix still uses its own yazelix subdirectory, and that declarative source reuse is not ambient-config adoption.

## Scope

- Provide one concise, current Home Manager or nix-darwin example for propagating appropriate XDG variables on macOS.
- Avoid prescribing an unsafe persistent XDG_RUNTIME_DIR; explain its lifecycle requirements or omit it from the general example.
- Provide representative shared-source examples for a safe native file such as Helix languages.toml or a Yazi override.
- Explain which Yazelix integration-owned configuration cannot be replaced wholesale, especially managed Zellij nodes and required Yazi or Helix bindings.
- Link the canonical Nova Home Manager text/source options and ownership diagnostics.
- Keep the documentation platform-aware and use public Yazelix Nova names after canonical cutover.

## Guardrails

- Add no runtime setting, global launchctl mutation, ambient-config reader, import side effect, or compatibility mode.
- Do not tell Yazelix to move, overwrite, or take ownership of native tool configuration.
- Do not imply that setting XDG_CONFIG_HOME makes ambient tool files control the managed runtime.

## Verification

- The documented Nix example evaluates on the intended macOS Home Manager or nix-darwin surface, or the untestable platform gap is stated explicitly.
- Paths and option names match the canonical Nova Home Manager module.
- The shared-source example installs the same immutable source at two explicit destinations without duplicate file content.
- Documentation clearly distinguishes XDG base selection, Yazelix namespacing, declarative source reuse, and ambient configuration.

title: Adopt Mars localhost and URL link-detection fix
description:
## Why

The Yazelix runtime packages Mars as its terminal, and current dogfooding reports that some printed links remain non-clickable, with localhost-style URLs suspected. Mars bead `yzt-harden-localhost-url-link-detection-5jw` owns reproduction, regex and hit-span tests, and the terminal fix. This mirror owns only publication and adoption of the corrected Mars revision in Yazelix.

## Child owner

- Mars repository: `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/mars`
- Owning bead: `yzt-harden-localhost-url-link-detection-5jw`
- Previous completed Mars feature: `yzt-t6w7.2`

## User-visible contract

In the packaged Yazelix runtime, URLs printed by development servers and ordinary terminal tools are fully clickable through the existing Mars gesture. The exact reported failures and representative localhost, loopback, HTTPS, punctuation, wrapper, and OSC 8 controls behave the same in standalone Mars and Mars launched through Yazelix.

## Scope

In scope:
- Wait for the owning Mars bead to reproduce the failure, add the focused regex/hit-span matrix, pass Mars checks, and publish a child revision.
- Update only the Yazelix Mars input lock needed to consume that published revision.
- Update `CHANGELOG.md` for the user-visible runtime fix and the README LOC scorecard required by repository policy.
- Run the exact flake, build, temporary-profile, runtime-refresh, and fresh-session dogfood checks.
- Record the exact previously failing strings and the adopted Mars revision in this bead before closing.

Out of scope:
- Duplicating URL regexes, link tests, opener logic, mouse handling, or terminal hint policy in Yazelix.
- Changing Zellij mouse policy, keybindings, desktop openers, Home Manager scope, or unrelated Mars settings.
- Expanding link behavior beyond the contract chosen and tested by the Mars owner bead.
- Updating the lock before the Mars revision is published and verified.

## Implementation Notes

1. Treat Mars bead `yzt-harden-localhost-url-link-detection-5jw` as the sole behavior owner even though cross-repository Beads dependencies cannot be encoded in this tracker.
2. Review the Mars commit, scorecard entry, focused regex tests, false-positive cases, and manual standalone evidence.
3. Update the locked Mars input without unrelated lock churn.
4. Refresh the installed Yazelix runtime only after the flake and package checks pass.
5. Dogfood the exact failing strings inside the packaged `yzx` workspace.

## Verification

- The owning Mars bead is closed with a published revision and recorded focused/full test evidence.
- `nix flake check` exits 0.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` evaluates the advertised outputs.
- `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs` exits 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix --profile <tmp>` installs successfully.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix` updates the user runtime to the verified revision.
- A fresh Yazelix session activates every previously failing raw string plus representative localhost, loopback, HTTPS, punctuation, and OSC 8 controls.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the Mars bead is not closed or the child revision is not published.
- Stop if the failing string works in standalone Mars but not through Yazelix; capture Zellij input and opener evidence before changing either repo.
- Stop if adoption requires main-repository link parsing, regex tests, or mouse-policy duplication.
- Stop before unrelated flake-lock, runtime, Home Manager, or configuration changes.

## Maintenance Notes

Owner split: Mars owns detection, extraction, click hit spans, and regression tests; Yazelix owns the locked child revision and packaged-runtime proof.

Effort: XS after the Mars fix. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH in the ownership split.

Rejected alternatives: implementing a second link detector in Yazelix, reopening the broad completed link feature, or adopting an unverified child commit.
close_reason: Adopted and packaged Mars bd6262cac3; all Yazelix verification gates pass

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Adoption verified at Mars revision bd6262cac3459a017b1e9b05fdb77019b47f8836. The clean rewritten URL owner is 2177a794e7, with link-gesture ownership in 9dc93ee46e. Recorded regressions were HTTP://localhost:4321/ failing to match, abchttp://localhost:3000 matching inside a word, and ➜ Local: http://localhost:4321/ producing byte-based rather than terminal-column hit spans. Punctuation-only matches and configured OSC 8 actions are covered too. Bare localhost or loopback host:port without a scheme remains deliberately unsupported. Yazelix already locked this exact Mars tip, so no duplicate integration code or lock churn was needed. Verification passed: nix flake check, nix flake show --all-systems, nix build .#yazelix, fresh temporary-profile installation, and installed runtime upgrade from 983a2892 to 5aa66b15. GUI activation was not duplicated here because Mars owns and passed the behavior matrix.

title: Clear hidden-tab agent activity without focusing it
description:
Why
Nova's base agent-tab-decoration feature needs a separate correctness boundary for activity that ends while its tab is hidden or unfocused. Classic sometimes left the busy marker visible until the user focused that tab. Focus must not be an acknowledgement mechanism for agent state.

User-visible contract
If agent activity ends on tab A while tab B is focused, tab A's activity decoration clears promptly without focusing or visiting tab A. The same rule applies when the agent popup is hidden, moved with its tab, or exits.

Dependency
This regression is meaningful only after yazelix-fix-agent-tab-rename-vo44-on5 establishes a truthful Nova activity signal and pipe-backed tab decoration.

Current sources of truth
- yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator owns per-pane activity facts, reduces them to an all-tab snapshot keyed by tab id, and publishes pipe_tab_activity.
- terminal_title_activity_state already removes activity when a spinner-prefixed title disappears.
- Classic's stale-state history showed that focus-driven refreshes and broad asynchronous polling are brittle and can cause pane walking or excess CPU.

Implementation scope
- Prove that the activity source chosen by the base feature emits an idle/end transition for an agent on an unfocused tab, including a hidden or suppressed popup.
- Feed that transition through the existing pane-activity facts and pipe snapshot; do not add a second activity owner.
- Preserve tab-id correctness when tabs move.
- Clear state on agent exit and pane close.
- Keep native tab names and rename mode independent of activity state.
- Do not use focus, tab visitation, shelling out to list-panes, process-lifetime heuristics, or broad polling/timers as the fix.

Focused verification
- Deterministic regression: mark an agent active on tab A, focus tab B, end activity on A, and assert the published snapshot becomes idle for A without a focus event for A.
- Cover tab movement, hidden popup, and process-exit/pane-close cleanup where those paths differ.
- Run focused child-repo tests, full project checks, and manually dogfood a multi-tab agent session.

Stop conditions
- If the base feature's selected signal cannot emit an end transition while hidden/unfocused, fix or split that signal source before adding refresh machinery.
- Do not approximate idle by elapsed time or require the user to visually acknowledge the marker.
acceptance_criteria:
- Agent activity ending on an unfocused tab removes its decoration without focusing that tab.
- Hidden/suppressed popup, tab movement, and agent exit or pane close do not leave stale activity.
- The fix uses the base feature's single activity owner and pipe snapshot, with no focus acknowledgement, native-name mutation, shellout polling, or timer heuristic.
- A deterministic regression test proves the unfocused end transition, and focused/full runtime checks pass.

title: Make packaged configuration discoverable through Overview and All
description:
## Why

Ratconfig joins locally owned finite schemas, child-published catalogs, curated native-tool fields, default documents, sparse user documents, and native-file actions. Those sources do not support one universal writable schema, and asking Yazelix to reproduce every type, default, and constraint would make it a duplicate semantic owner.

Users need one predictable overview, complete discovery where an owner can publish it, honest native escape hatches where it cannot, preserved sparse intent, and inline editing only when the source integration can validate and persist safely.

## Outcome

Every packaged configuration surface is discoverable through the strongest contract its real owner publishes.

- Overview contains optional owner/product recommendations plus every non-inherited field that needs attention: explicit, invalid, or externally managed.
- All contains the complete inventory for finite or machine-readable owner catalogs and every deliberately exposed field for curated partial-schema integrations.
- Search spans All.
- Each modeled setting distinguishes the inherited baseline, explicit or invalid intent, effective value, and origin where the host can resolve them.
- An explicit value equal to the baseline remains explicit; reset removes intent and recomputes the value.
- Generic rows, diagnostics, ownership explanations, and exact file actions cover dynamic or opaque native state.
- Safe owner-backed values may be edited inline; complex or unsupported values remain read-only and open their native source.
- Native configuration files remain canonical.

## Definitions

- Baseline: the value and origin that would be effective if the selected override were absent. It may combine tool fallback and lower-priority Yazelix packaged layers.
- Intent: the sparse assertion present in the user or externally managed layer, including an explicit value equal to the baseline.
- Effective: the resolved value the runtime uses, when determinable.
- Default document: evidence for packaged values, observed paths, grouping, ordering, and syntactic shapes; never proof of a complete schema or safe edit contract.
- Capability: a host-declared interaction backed by owner validation and safe persistence. Type or renderability alone does not grant it.

## Ownership rules

1. Consume a versioned machine-readable catalog when the actual configuration owner publishes one.
2. Keep finite Yazelix-owned schemas in Yazelix.
3. For native formats without a stable catalog, expose the smallest truthful curated subset plus generic document and file surfaces; do not manufacture completeness with a local mirror.
4. Ratconfig owns generic sparse-intent presentation, Overview/All, search, rendering, diagnostics, and declared-capability interaction—not child-tool semantics.
5. Yazelix owns source discovery, layer resolution, the local root schema, cross-tool integration/apply policy, optional recommendations, and host persistence routing—not copied child paths, types, defaults, constraints, or validators.
6. Inline editing is a declared capability of a source integration, never an inference from a literal default, type label, or renderability.
7. Reset removes the selected sparse override. Silent fallback or baseline materialization is not an acceptable substitute.
8. Preserve explicit and invalid user input truthfully, including explicit values equal to the current baseline.

## Scope

- Consume ratconfig-h7d through mirror child .13 and replace Core/current/default ambiguity with Overview plus sparse intent, baseline, effective value, origin, and editing capability.
- Review root/popups, Mars, Cursors, Zellij, Starship, Helix, Yazi, and Nushell using the correct owner class.
- Prove parity only for owner-published finite catalogs.
- Use default documents for observed baseline discovery without claiming supported-but-absent settings or semantic metadata they do not contain.
- For partial or dynamic surfaces, provide honest file actions, document rows, opaque diagnostics, and ownership guidance. A wholly invalid native document remains a source-level diagnostic and exact file action unless the owner can localize the error safely.
- Keep complex or unsafe values read-only when the source cannot edit them safely.
- Preserve sparse native files, invalid input where safe, Home Manager ownership, validation, and existing runtime apply behavior.
- Update focused checks, current-state docs, CHANGELOG, and the LOC scorecard as user-visible slices land.

## Out of scope

- No copied child schema, scraped documentation inventory, universal configuration language, private config database, generic watcher, compatibility layer, config generator, or automatic migration.
- No requirement that every native format become a field editor.
- No arbitrary first-ten or alphabetical recommendation algorithm.
- No pure-text inline semantics that move parsing and serialization into every host.
- No yzx config CLI commands in this program; the user postponed that separate automation surface.

## Verification

Each child names its inventory, baseline, intent, validation, persistence, and effective-value sources and proves representative Overview, All, search, explicit-same-as-baseline, externally managed visibility, reset/unset with inherited-value provenance, localized versus document-level invalid visibility, edit capability, native-file fallback, and ownership behavior appropriate to that source.

The final gate proves the joined product contract without asserting fake field-count parity for partial or dynamic surfaces. Full flake, package, and temporary-profile checks pass before completion.

## Stop conditions

Stop if a completeness claim lacks a versioned owner. Stop if implementation copies a child schema into Yazelix without a separately chosen ownership transfer. Stop if Overview hides any non-inherited intent, including externally managed state. Stop if All claims completeness for opaque state. Stop if inferred syntax or type alone authorizes editing. Stop if reset writes a baseline value. Stop if tool fallback, packaged baseline, intent, and effective state are collapsed into ambiguous current/default fields.

## Rejected and deferred alternatives

Rejected: one umbrella schema in Yazelix, forcing every native format into field rows, a permanently read-only Ratconfig, pure-text inline editing, and a blind first-ten overview. Direct native editing remains the universal fallback rather than the whole product. CLI get/set/unset/edit/validate commands are deferred until after the TUI architecture is implemented and dogfooded.
notes:
Architecture decision refined by the sparse-intent model used by mature settings and declarative systems: absence means inherit; explicit presence means the user relies on that value, even when it currently equals the baseline. The closed root/popups slice remains valid and its existing selection becomes recommendations. Mars remains the decisive owner-catalog vertical slice. If Mars still requires per-field Yazelix schema logic, stop before propagating the architecture.

Further research supports treating Ratconfig as a workbench and Yazelix as the orchestrator. Prefer an owner-published catalog, parser, or constrained command/API as the conduit; guarded native patching is the fallback, not a new universal lens system. Keep provenance bounded: show the origin needed to explain effective state and what reset will inherit, but do not build full override history. An accepted value is not necessarily operationally correct, so apply and runtime status must remain truthful.

title: Consume Ratconfig Core and All settings release
description:
## Why

Yazelix must consume the generic Core/All contract from Ratconfig before per-config inventories can use one consistent view, count, toggle, and search behavior. Ratconfig v5.0.0 is published at 79999f6722d114cebae4944f104daa7683459400, so Yazelix can consume the released contract directly.

## Evidence

- flake.nix:50 declares Ratconfig as a pinned non-flake input.
- flake.lock owns the exact Ratconfig revision and currently contains unrelated user work that must be preserved.
- crates/yzx-config/src/model.rs constructs one ConfigUiModel for all tabs.
- Existing bead yazelix-5vl-064 owns consumption and dogfooding of the earlier Ratconfig revision c6ee666.
- Ratconfig bead ratconfig-core-all-settings-5d9.3 closed with annotated tag v5.0.0 at 79999f6722d114cebae4944f104daa7683459400.

## User-Visible Contract

Ratconfig opens in Core when a tab has non-core settings. Normal-mode a toggles Core and All. Search spans All and reveals matching non-core fields without changing the saved view. Explicit or invalid non-core values remain visible in Core. The UI uses Core and All terminology only.

## Scope

In scope:
- Pin Ratconfig v5.0.0 at exact revision 79999f6722d114cebae4944f104daa7683459400.
- Preserve the accepted visual behavior from closed bead yazelix-5vl-064, which v5.0.0 includes.
- Update only the Ratconfig input to the exact released revision while preserving unrelated lockfile changes.
- Populate the required ConfigUiModel::core_fields field directly; add no legacy adapter or compatibility path.
- Keep file actions, sections, native TOML rows, Home Manager ownership, save/reset routing, and apply statuses working.
- Update README controls, configuration docs, CHANGELOG, and the LOC scorecard.
- Refresh the installed profile after full verification.

Out of scope:
- No per-tool complete inventory or final Core classification in this bead.
- No local Ratconfig source edits, vendoring, path override, or duplicated UI filtering.
- No change to config persistence or runtime live-apply policy.

## Implementation Notes

1. Use the published v5.0.0 tag and verify its exact revision before editing the lock.
2. Update the pin from the closed visual revision to v5.0.0 without changing unrelated inputs.
3. Initially mark existing structured fields Core so adoption is behavior-preserving; per-config beads expand All and refine Core.
4. Add integration checks for toggle, all-scope search, explicit non-core visibility, and terminology.

## Verification

- jq -r .nodes.ratconfig.locked.rev flake.lock prints the exact released revision.
- nix flake check exits 0.
- nix flake show --all-systems exits 0.
- nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs exits 0.
- nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix --profile a-temporary-profile exits 0.
- nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix succeeds after acceptance.
- Focused yzx-config tests prove Core/All toggle and search integration.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if tag v5.0.0 does not resolve to 79999f6722d114cebae4944f104daa7683459400.
- Stop if the new revision omits the visual fixes owned by yazelix-5vl-064.
- Stop if updating flake.lock would overwrite unrelated user-owned input changes.
- Stop if integration requires a Yazelix copy of Ratconfig filtering or rendering.

## Maintenance Notes

Published child release: Ratconfig v5.0.0 at 79999f6722d114cebae4944f104daa7683459400. Update the existing Ratconfig pin only; add no crate, adapter, shim, or dual-version path.
notes:
Ratconfig v5 API audit handoff 2026-07-15: v5 adds required public fields ConfigUiModel::core_fields and ConfigUiApp::settings_view. Yazelix has one direct ConfigUiModel literal at crates/yzx-config/src/model.rs:103 and no direct ConfigUiApp literal, so adoption must populate core_fields and can continue using ConfigUiApp::new. Yazelix is the only consumer; add no legacy adapter, compatibility shim, or dual-version path. Consume the v5 API directly after the exact tag is published.

Release gate cleared 2026-07-15: Ratconfig annotated tag v5.0.0 peels to 79999f6722d114cebae4944f104daa7683459400. Reactivated for direct Yazelix adoption.
close_reason: Pinned Ratconfig v5.0.0 at 79999f6722d114cebae4944f104daa7683459400, adopted the Core allowlist API with all current fields classified Core, documented the controls, and verified Core/All toggle, All-scope search, explicit visibility, full flake checks, package builds, and profile installation.

title: Prove honest Overview and All configuration coverage
description:
## Why

Per-surface work can still leave inconsistent terminology, search behavior, ownership guidance, or false completeness claims. One final gate should prove the joined Ratconfig experience while respecting that finite catalogs, curated integrations, and dynamic native documents have different owners and verification boundaries.

## User-visible contract

Every packaged configuration surface is reachable and searchable through the strongest honest discovery surface it supports. Overview contains reviewed product settings plus every non-inherited value that needs attention: explicit, invalid, or externally managed. All is field-complete where a versioned owner catalog exists and contains every Yazelix-curated field where it does not. Native file actions, generic document rows, preserved opaque entries, and read-only ownership messages cover the remaining state without pretending Yazelix owns its schema. No UI or documentation uses guided as the category term.

## Scope

In scope:
- Consolidate a focused packaged contract over every inventory/discovery source.
- Verify owner-catalog parity only for finite machine-readable catalogs.
- Verify normal-mode Overview/All toggle, All-scope search, explicit, invalid, and externally managed non-recommended visibility, sections, file actions, and Home Manager ownership.
- Verify representative simple edits, complex read-only rows, opaque preserved state, reset previews plus unset behavior, sparse writes, owner validation, and truthful runtime/apply status.
- Verify each partial or dynamic surface documents its native escape hatch and does not claim exhaustive field coverage.
- Reconcile architecture and configuration docs with actual owners.
- Update CHANGELOG and README LOC scorecard, run full checks, and refresh the installed profile after acceptance.

Out of scope:
- No new setting, default, runtime feature, umbrella schema, adapter, or config owner.
- No literal expected list duplicating an owner catalog.
- No GUI launch unless separately requested.

## Implementation notes

Prefer owner-to-visible parity for published catalogs and representative behavior for curated/dynamic surfaces. The gate should fail if an owner-published field cannot render, a curated field lacks classification, a native surface lacks a truthful escape hatch, or docs overstate completeness. Do not compare Zellij, Helix, Nushell, or another dynamic surface to a fabricated host inventory.

## Verification

Run yzx-config tests, nix flake check, nix flake show --all-systems, the Yazelix and runtime builds, a clean temporary profile install, and git diff --check. Refresh the user profile only after acceptance.

## Stop conditions

Stop while any config-surface child remains incomplete. Stop if a completeness claim lacks owner evidence. Stop if checks become source-literal mirrors instead of behavior or owner parity contracts. Stop if unrelated dirty work cannot be preserved.

## Rejected alternatives

Rejected: a universal field-count assertion, a second umbrella schema, and treating a native-file action as insufficient merely because the native owner has no stable catalog API.


## Architecture constraint

Overview is a generic attention surface, not a second schema: it combines optional recommended identities with every non-inherited field. The configuration owner remains authoritative for paths, types, defaults, constraints, and writable semantics. Inline editing is allowed only when this integration has owner-backed validation and safe persistence; otherwise keep the value read-only and expose its exact native-file action. This child depends on the Ratconfig Overview integration in .13 unless it is already a completed focused slice.

title: Verify live apply timing for Mars settings
description:
## Why

Ratconfig currently tells users that every exposed Mars setting except mars.appearance.preset applies on the next launch. Maintainer dogfooding suggests more of these settings update the existing Mars window live. Incorrect apply timing makes users restart unnecessarily and weakens trust in the Takes effect column.

## Evidence

- crates/yzx-config/src/model.rs:55-64 assigns every MARS_FIELDS row through mars_apply_status.
- crates/yzx-config/src/model.rs:420-430 returns live only for mars.appearance.preset and next launch for every other Mars path.
- crates/yzx-config/src/catalog.rs:349-363 exposes window width, height, opacity, font size, line height, scrollbar, and audio/visual bell settings.
- ../mars/frontends/rioterm/src/watcher.rs:8-57 watches the config directory and emits a debounced reload event.
- ../mars/frontends/rioterm/src/application.rs:383-438 reloads config, updates every open route, and reconfigures each window.
- ../mars/frontends/rioterm/src/screen/mod.rs:467-555 reapplies font size, line height, renderer state, dimensions, and window opacity behavior.
- ../mars/frontends/rioterm/src/renderer/mod.rs:145-160 rebuilds scrollbar state from the reloaded enable-scroll-bar value.
- ../mars/frontends/rioterm/src/application.rs:571-580 reads audio and visual bell flags from the current in-memory config when BEL arrives.
- ../mars/frontends/rioterm/src/router/window.rs:145-163 applies width and height in the new-window builder, with no corresponding resize in configure_window, so those two may remain launch-scoped.
- ARCHITECTURE.md:395-396 and docs/configuration.md:233-234 currently claim every non-palette Mars field is next-launch only.

## User-Visible Contract

Each Mars setting displays the narrowest truthful apply timing for the exact packaged Mars revision. Settings proven to update an already-open Mars window say live. Settings consumed only when a window is created retain next launch or an equally precise new-window label. The UI and documentation do not generalize one result across unrelated fields.

## Scope

In scope:
- On the exact Mars revision pinned by flake.lock, change each exposed setting through yzx config and observe the already-open Mars window.
- Verify mars.appearance.preset, window.width, window.height, window.opacity, fonts.size, line-height, enable-scroll-bar, bell.audio, and bell.visual independently.
- Exercise the behavior needed to observe each setting: window geometry, opacity, font metrics, line spacing, scroll activity, and an explicit BEL for both bell modes.
- Distinguish watcher pickup, current-window application, and newly launched-window application.
- Replace mars_apply_status with the smallest evidence-backed per-path classification and add one focused mapping/behavior contract.
- Reconcile ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/configuration.md, CHANGELOG.md, and the README LOC scorecard if user-visible labels change.

Out of scope:
- No Ratconfig API or renderer change; Yazelix owns apply policy and product text.
- No Mars runtime behavior change in this bead.
- No claim about settings outside the exact packaged Mars inventory or untested operating systems/compositors.
- No weak test that only mirrors a newly introduced literal table without defending the user-visible grouping.

## Implementation Notes

1. Record a small evidence matrix for every field: watcher reload observed, current window changed, new window changed, and platform/session details.
2. Treat width and height as separate from visual/render settings; source inspection suggests they are startup-only.
3. If behavior differs by platform or compositor, use precise guidance instead of labeling the field universally live.
4. If a setting should apply live but the pinned Mars implementation does not, keep Yazelix truthful and create a Mars-owned follow-up rather than hiding a runtime change here.

## Verification

- Manual dogfooding against the flake-pinned Mars revision records one result for all nine exposed settings; GUI launch requires maintainer approval when this bead is implemented.
- cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzx-config/Cargo.toml exits 0 with a focused apply-status contract.
- nix flake check exits 0.
- nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs exits 0.
- git diff --check exits 0.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the tested Mars binary does not match flake.lock.
- Stop if a live result cannot be distinguished from a newly created window or config-UI-only palette update.
- Stop if truthful classification requires modifying Mars; create a separate Mars bead and preserve the observed Yazelix label.
- Stop if unrelated dirty flake.lock work cannot be preserved.

## Maintenance Notes

Effort S, risk LOW, confidence HIGH that the current blanket label is too broad; confidence is MEDIUM about the exact per-field result until manual dogfooding. Add no crates. This verification should block the Mars Core/All inventory bead because that bead retains Yazelix-owned apply policy.
notes:
Implementation staged against Mars revision 5a652a77aa8fe2ccc1edd9e5686d51f824053f06. Source evidence: the config watcher reloads the complete Config, update_config reapplies opacity, font size, line height, renderer/scrollbar state, and current bell flags to open routes; window width and height are read only by the new-window builder. Classification: live = mars.appearance.preset, window.opacity, fonts.size, line-height, enable-scroll-bar, bell.audio, bell.visual; new windows = window.width, window.height. Verification passed: rustfmt, the flake-pinned yzx package build with 40 yzx-config tests, nix flake check, nix flake show --all-systems, nix build .#yazelix, temporary-profile install, git diff --check, and installed profile upgrade. Direct local cargo test is temporarily incompatible with the unpublished sibling Ratconfig Core/All work; the pinned flake test lane passes. Manual COSMIC/Wayland dogfooding remains before closure.

Fresh-eyes and isomorphic simplification pass: renamed the focused test so it no longer overclaims direct binary verification, replaced two filtered summary lists with one ordered nine-field contract, and reduced Rust by 3 lines while strengthening completeness. Rejected a generic apply-status helper or timing enum because each adds a concept for negligible LOC savings and the current match remains the single clear owner. Post-pass rustfmt, full flake check, all-system flake show, package build, temporary-profile install, diff check, scorecard, and installed runtime refresh pass.

Second fresh-eyes/simplification pass: replaced eight repeated ConfigUiApplyStatus constructions and the one-caller next-launch wrapper in model.rs with one local apply_status constructor; every rendered summary, label, detail, and pending=false value is unchanged. Simplified the Mars timing test to validate the exact field set and summaries without accidentally freezing catalog row order or allocating a vector. Tightened CHANGELOG wording from the overbroad font metrics to font size and line height. Net reduction versus the previous reviewed shape: 25 Rust lines and one specialized helper concept. Rejected moving the helper across modules for the lone Yazi constructor, direct match-arm construction, and collapsing bell/render details because each increases coupling/repetition or loses useful precision. Full post-pass verification and installed runtime refresh passed; manual dogfood remains.

Third fresh-eyes/simplification pass: removed “immediately” from the live Mars detail text because the config watcher deliberately debounces reloads; “live in open windows” is the precise contract without a zero-latency claim. Revalidated the local apply_status helper against Ratconfig: all eight model call sites represent persisted, non-pending state, so the helper removes a repeated invariant rather than hiding meaningful variation. Rejected moving timing metadata into the generic field catalog, sharing the helper across modules for one Yazi call site, and merging the focused completeness test into a broader model test; each would add coupling or weaken the regression boundary without removing a concept. Full flake check, all-system show, package build, temporary-profile install/status, diff/format checks, scorecard, and installed runtime refresh pass. Manual Mars dogfood remains before closure.
close_reason: Completed: source-backed per-setting apply labels shipped, all automated gates passed, and maintainer dogfooding confirmed the live Mars behavior.

title: Expose packaged Zellij themes in Ratconfig
description:
## Why

Nova's Zellij tab cannot select a theme even though the pinned Yazelix Zellij 0.44.3 package already embeds 41 named themes. A focused picker would make a high-value native preference discoverable without copying theme files or introducing another theme owner.

## Evidence

- `crates/yzx-config/src/catalog.rs:382`: the guarded sidecar forbids integration-owned nodes, but `theme` is not forbidden.
- `crates/yzx-config/src/catalog.rs:395`: the eight editable Zellij fields do not include `theme`.
- `crates/yzx-config/src/model.rs:84`: every exposed Zellij field expects an effective packaged default.
- `defaults/zellij/config.kdl:1`: the packaged config does not currently select an explicit theme.
- `runtime/yzx-zellij-config.rs:41`: safe sidecar assignments are appended to the packaged config and materialized as the runtime config.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij/zellij-utils/src/consts.rs:24`: the pinned fork embeds its theme assets in the Zellij binary.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij/zellij-utils/src/setup.rs:27`: Zellij loads every embedded theme into its default theme set.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij/zellij-utils/src/input/options.rs:48`: `theme` is a native scalar Zellij option.

## User-Visible Contract

Ratconfig's Zellij tab exposes a Overview `theme` setting with `default` plus every theme embedded by the pinned Yazelix Zellij package as direct choices. Saving writes only a sparse `theme "<name>"` sidecar assignment; reset removes that override and restores the packaged default. The setting reports honestly whether the active session applies the change live or needs a new session.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add the native scalar `theme` field to the existing Zellij sidecar/config UI owner.
- Make `default` plus the 41 themes embedded in the pinned fork available as picker choices.
- Give the packaged config an explicit behavior-preserving `theme "default"` value so current/default/reset semantics remain total.
- Preserve save, reset, active-runtime patching, surrounding KDL, and integration-node guards.
- Add one version-parity check that compares the exposed packaged choices with the pinned fork's `zellij-utils/assets/themes/*.kdl` names.
- Update configuration docs, runtime notes, CHANGELOG, and the README LOC scorecard for the user-visible setting.

Out of scope:
- Do not copy theme KDL assets into this repository; the Zellij fork remains their single owner.
- Do not add or edit themes in the Zellij fork.
- Do not add custom theme authoring, a managed theme directory, previews, or terminal/Mars/Helix/Yazi theme synchronization.
- Do not add `theme_dark` / `theme_light` automatic appearance switching in this slice.
- Do not absorb the complete Zellij settings inventory owned by `yazelix-5vl-core-all-config-discovery-lsw.5`.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add `theme` through the existing `ZELLIJ_FIELDS`, sidecar parser, writer, reset, and model path; do not create a second config surface.
2. Treat the pinned fork's embedded asset names as the availability source of truth. Keep only chooser metadata in Nova and make drift fail in the flake check.
3. Include `default` explicitly in addition to asset-derived names.
4. Add one focused contract test covering parse, choice validation, sparse save, reset, and preservation of neighboring assignments.
5. Confirm active-session behavior manually. Label it live only if the pinned watcher visibly applies it; otherwise label it next-session without adding reload machinery.
6. Preserve the runtime's existing acceptance boundary for hand-edited sidecars. If Ratconfig's exhaustive choice model would newly invalidate a previously working custom theme name, stop and record the smallest host/child capability needed instead of silently narrowing the runtime contract.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzx-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 and prove theme parse/save/reset plus neighboring KDL preservation.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and prove chooser/theme-asset parity, sidecar guard parity, LOC accounting, and existing contracts.
- `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0 with the packaged default accepted by the pinned Zellij.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix --profile <tmp>` should install the changed package.
- Manual dogfood: use `yzx config` to select two visually distinct packaged themes, verify the documented current/next-session behavior, reset, and confirm the default returns.
- After runtime changes, `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix` should update the installed runtime.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the pinned package does not actually load all cited embedded assets.
- Stop if implementation starts copying theme contents or creating a second theme owner.
- Stop if exposing choices requires weakening Zellij integration-node guards.
- Stop if the first slice expands into cross-application theme synchronization or custom theme management.
- Stop if cited current-state code has drifted materially or exact verification fails twice for the same reason after a reasonable fix attempt.

## Maintenance Notes

The package-vs-in-house decision is settled: reuse Zellij's embedded assets and existing Rust/std sidecar owner; add no crate or package. This is a focused slice under the Overview/All config program. The broader Zellij inventory should count this completed field in its coverage rather than reimplement it. Rejected for this slice: copied theme files, a Nova theme registry, previews, and adaptive dark/light pairs. Effort S/M, risk LOW/MED, confidence HIGH.

## Architecture constraint

Overview is a generic attention surface, not a second schema: it combines optional recommended identities with every explicit or invalid field. The configuration owner remains authoritative for paths, types, defaults, constraints, and writable semantics. Inline editing is allowed only when this integration has owner-backed validation and safe persistence; otherwise keep the value read-only and expose its exact native-file action. This child depends on the Ratconfig Overview integration in .13 unless it is already a completed focused slice.
notes:
Implemented Ratconfig Zellij theme selection with a virtual default plus the 41 theme identities declared by the pinned Yazelix Zellij fork. Selecting default removes the sparse theme assignment from both the native sidecar and watched active-session config; packaged selections apply live in managed sessions. Native custom theme names remain accepted and a current custom value is retained as a picker choice. The main repo tracks only chooser metadata, with a flake check proving exact identity parity against the fork assets. Fresh-eyes review fixed an edge case where reset returned before refreshing the watched runtime when the sidecar was already absent. Simplification removed redundant hard-coded inventory counts and collapsed choice construction to one allocation pass. A second review traced the fresh-session launch path and confirmed active_zellij_config always materializes the watched state config even without a sidecar. It then strengthened and simplified parity by comparing the tracked manifest directly with sorted fork identities, so picker order cannot drift while removing an unnecessary expected file. Simplifying the nesting-aware KDL identity parser or piping its loop into sort was rejected because either could miss multi-theme assets or mask awk failures. Automated acceptance passed: formatting, 42 Rust tests, strict Clippy, package build, all host flake checks, flake show/all-systems, and isolated profile install. Installed runtime already contains the runtime code; manual live-session theme switching remains.

Architecture refinement: theme remains a valid curated Yazelix field because its finite choices are checked directly against assets embedded by the pinned Zellij fork; that parity check is not a native-schema mirror. Do not close this bead until yazelix-5vl-hkk replaces the current default_mode-specific preservation work with the generic opaque-scalar boundary and the live theme path is dogfooded against that simplified result.
close_reason: Implemented, fully verified, and accepted through live runtime dogfooding

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Maintainer dogfooding confirms packaged Zellij theme selection works in the installed runtime after the generic opaque-scalar preservation fix. This clears the remaining live-session acceptance gate.

title: Consume Ratconfig sparse-intent Overview contract
description:
## Purpose

Mirror Ratconfig bead ratconfig-h7d and make its released sparse-intent, capability-aware Overview/All contract the single generic configuration-discovery model consumed by Yazelix.

## User-visible contract

yzx config opens each settings tab on Overview when that tab has recommended fields or non-inherited fields distinct from its broader inventory. Non-inherited means explicit, invalid, or externally managed. a toggles Overview and All, and search spans All.

For every modeled setting Yazelix presents the strongest truthful state it can resolve:

- baseline: the effective value and origin with the selected user or managed override removed;
- intent: absent, explicitly present, invalid, or externally managed;
- effective: the value the runtime will use, when determinable;
- origin/owner: the layer responsible for the value;
- capability: the safe interaction available for that source.

An explicit value equal to the baseline remains explicit and visible. When known, the reset/inherit action previews the value and origin that will become effective. Reset removes the sparse override and recomputes the effective value; it never writes the current baseline into the user file. Localized invalid values remain visible with diagnostics instead of silently falling back. A wholly invalid document remains a source-level diagnostic with the exact native-file action unless the owner can localize it safely.

Safe owner-backed values retain inline controls. Complex, opaque, unsupported, or read-only values remain inspectable and open the exact native source where one exists. The completed root/popups recommendation selection remains user-visible after the terminology and state-model change.

## Sources of truth

- Root: defaults/config.toml and the local root validator define Yazelix's finite schema and lower-priority packaged baseline; the sparse user file records intent.
- Mars and other catalog owners: the exact pinned catalog or parser defines supported paths, semantic types, defaults, constraints, and writable shapes.
- Native default documents: provide packaged baseline values and observed paths/shapes only; they do not prove completeness or writability.
- User and Home Manager documents: preserve explicit intent and ownership, including explicit values equal to the baseline.
- Runtime materializers and apply paths: determine effective values and apply timing.

## Ownership

- Ratconfig owns generic intent/baseline/effective presentation, Overview/All filtering, counts, search, rendering, and declared-capability interaction.
- Yazelix owns source discovery, layer resolution, its local root schema, cross-tool integration/apply policy, optional recommendations, host validation/persistence routing, and truthful origin labels.
- Child tools own their paths, semantic types, defaults, constraints, and writable behavior.
- Native files remain canonical; Yazelix must not mirror child catalogs or create a second config store.

## Scope

- Wait for ratconfig-h7d to publish one exact revision and update the pinned dependency.
- Migrate ConfigUiModel integration from Core/current/default semantics to Overview plus sparse intent, baseline, effective value, and origin/ownership.
- Rename local recommendation constants and tests where they imply child semantic ownership.
- Map editing from explicit host capabilities, not type labels. Preserve current toggles, choices, ordered lists, free text, and reset only where the owning parser and persistence path make them safe.
- Treat inferred default-document syntax as display evidence only. Do not infer completeness, ranges, enum choices, units, apply timing, or writability from a literal default.
- Preserve All-scope search, a toggling, exact file actions, generic document rows, diagnostics, Home Manager read-only ownership, sparse writes, validation, theme behavior, and apply timing.
- Preserve invalid and unknown native input when the existing source contract can do so safely; never normalize it silently to a baseline. Do not add tolerant or error-recovering parsers solely to manufacture field rows from an unparseable document.
- Update current-state docs, CHANGELOG, and README LOC scorecard.

## Verification

Use focused behavioral contracts:

- One inherited root field follows the packaged baseline.
- One explicit root field equal to its baseline remains explicit and visible in Overview.
- Changing a test baseline affects the inherited field but not the explicitly pinned field.
- Reset previews the inherited value and origin, deletes the sparse path, and reloads inherited state without materializing the baseline.
- One localized invalid explicit value remains visible with a diagnostic and is not silently replaced; one wholly invalid document produces a source-level diagnostic and exact file action without fabricated field recovery.
- One non-recommended Home Manager-managed value remains visible in Overview, reports external ownership, and remains read-only.
- One safe owner-backed scalar uses its declared control; one boolean-looking or structured value without write capability remains read-only and opens its native file.
- Search from Overview reaches an All-only field.
- Existing root/popups behavior remains equivalent apart from corrected wording and provenance.
- cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzx-config/Cargo.toml, nix flake check, nix flake show --all-systems, nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs, and a clean temporary profile install exit 0.
- Refresh the installed user profile only after acceptance.

## Stop conditions

- Stop if consumption requires a Core or old-state compatibility adapter.
- Stop if Yazelix derives recommendations from alphabetical or arbitrary first-N order. Stop if integration requires a second error-recovering parser to infer fields from wholly invalid native text.
- Stop if a child field becomes editable from inferred syntax or a type label without owner-backed validation and safe persistence.
- Stop if reset writes a baseline value rather than removing intent.
- Stop if the implementation collapses tool default, Yazelix packaged baseline, explicit user intent, and effective runtime value into one ambiguous default/current pair.
- Stop if the integration creates a CLI, config database, watcher, universal schema, or migration not required by ratconfig-h7d.

## Deferred alternative

The user explicitly chose to postpone yzx config CLI commands. Do not add get/set/unset/edit/validate commands in this bead. Reconsider that automation surface only as a later independently approved slice after the TUI architecture is dogfooded.
notes:
The first integration should prove behavior rather than invent a large provenance framework. "Baseline" means the resolved lower-priority value after removing the selected override; owner defaults may remain optional metadata if they differ. Preserve explicit-same-as-baseline because it pins intent across future packaged-default changes. Use the current native parsers and sparse patchers as write authorities.

Research refinement: Ratconfig is the workbench; Yazelix is the orchestrator and layer resolver. Overview visibility is recommended OR intent is not inherited. Provenance is intentionally minimal but must include the origin of the effective value and, when different, the baseline that reset will inherit. Prefer owner conduits and parsers; retain guarded native patchers only for bounded operations.

title: Review root and popup settings for Core
description:
## Why

The Yazelix root and popup schema is finite and owned locally, but Core/All adoption still needs an explicit completeness and classification pass. Dynamic custom popup ids are valid configured values even though their names cannot be known before users create them.

## Evidence

- defaults/config.toml owns packaged semantic defaults.
- crates/yzx-config/src/catalog.rs:212 defines CONFIG_FIELDS and splits them between Main and Popups.
- crates/yzx-config/src/root_config.rs:204 rejects unsupported root paths and delegates only popups.<id> to dynamic validation.
- crates/yzx-config/src/custom_popups.rs owns dynamic popup rendering and validation.
- ARCHITECTURE.md Root schema inventory documents the current finite fields and dynamic popup namespace.

## User-Visible Contract

All contains every supported finite root setting plus every configured custom popup field. Core contains the product-essential subset selected by this review and always includes explicit or invalid non-core values. Search finds any finite or configured dynamic path. Unknown root paths remain rejected rather than appearing as editable settings.

## Scope

In scope:
- Reconcile defaults/config.toml, CONFIG_FIELDS, root validation, architecture inventory, and Ratconfig rows into one complete checked inventory.
- Review every finite root field and each custom-popup field kind for Core membership using frequency, product impact, safety, and clarity.
- Mark non-core fields without removing them from All.
- Render configured dynamic popup entries discoverably, including complex values as read-only when needed.
- Preserve sparse persistence, validation, managed keybinding collision rules, and apply statuses.
- Record graduation decisions and rejected Core candidates in the close reason or Beads notes.

Out of scope:
- No new root setting, popup feature, command, or compatibility alias.
- No weakening of unknown-path rejection.
- No automatic creation of custom popup ids.

## Verification

- cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzx-config/Cargo.toml exits 0 and proves schema/catalog parity.
- Focused tests prove representative Core and non-core root fields, all-scope search, and configured custom popup discovery.
- nix flake check exits 0 after integration.
- The README LOC scorecard is refreshed for project-file changes.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the documented root inventory and validator disagree; resolve the one owner before classification.
- Stop if dynamic popup rendering would invent entries that are not configured.
- Stop if Core classification changes defaults or runtime behavior.

## Maintenance Notes

Dependency decision: use the existing local schema and Ratconfig metadata only. The review term is Core, never guided. Effort M, risk LOW/MED, confidence HIGH.
notes: Review outcome: ROOT_CONFIG_CORE_PATHS contains shell.program, editor.command, agent.command, welcome.enabled, welcome.style, all six managed action keybindings, and bar.widgets. All-only defaults are open.log_level, agent.args, welcome.duration_seconds, popup.side_margin, and popup.vertical_margin; explicit or invalid values still surface in Core through Ratconfig. Non-root inventories remain Core until their own review beads. Configured popups use Ratconfig's generic TOML document rows for the leaves actually present under popups.<id>; every discovered leaf is explicit and therefore remains visible in Core, while All still contains it. Absent optional leaves and unconfigured ids are not synthesized. Rejected: a Yazelix-side five-field UI schema and derived defaults per popup id, because it duplicated the existing validator and underused Ratconfig's generic passthrough.
close_reason: Completed: reviewed the finite root inventory, applied the 12-path Core classification, kept five advanced defaults All-only, and exposed configured custom-popup leaves through Ratconfig's generic TOML rows without synthesizing ids or duplicating the popup schema. Unknown paths, semantic validation, sparse writes, and non-root Core behavior remain intact. Verified with 48 yzx-config tests, nix flake check, all-system flake evaluation, full Yazelix build, temporary profile install, and installed runtime upgrade.

title: Expose all Mars settings and review Overview recommendations
description:
## Why

Mars supports a broad typed configuration surface that Ratconfig can generically display, but Yazelix currently exposes only nine locally mirrored MARS_FIELDS rows. The concrete regression is window.blur: the packaged Mars parser accepts it while a user who does not already know the native key cannot discover it. The exact packaged Mars revision must own completeness; Yazelix should own only which settings are prominent in the Yazelix product.

## Evidence

- defaults/mars/config.toml explicitly sets only a curated packaged base and is not a complete schema.
- crates/yzx-config/src/catalog.rs defines nine local Mars fields.
- crates/yzx-config/src/model.rs builds only those rows and currently marks every exposed row Overview.
- crates/yzx-config/src/native_config.rs validates writes only through the selected local FieldSpec.
- Mars bead yzt-ehg owns a lightweight versioned public config catalog. Yazelix mirror yazelix-5vl-f8d tracks its publication.
- Ratconfig v5 already owns generic scalar, structured, Overview, All, all-scope search, and explicit non-recommended visibility behavior.

## User-visible contract

All exposes every public entry in the exact pinned Mars catalog, including defaulted settings absent from packaged config.toml, structured collections, and platform overrides with truthful availability guidance. Internal or derived Mars state excluded by the owner catalog does not appear as user configuration.

Overview is a small Yazelix product filter. Every non-inherited non-recommended value remains visible in Overview, including explicit, invalid, and externally managed state; search spans All. No Mars-specific renderer or schema is added to Ratconfig.

## Agreed Overview recommendations

Yazelix owns exactly these initial 16 recommended identities:

- mars.appearance.preset
- window.width
- window.height
- window.mode
- window.decorations
- window.opacity
- window.opacity-cells
- window.blur
- fonts.family
- fonts.size
- line-height
- confirm-before-quit
- copy-on-select
- hide-mouse-cursor-when-typing
- bell.audio
- bell.visual

These are recommended because they have an understandable visible effect during ordinary Yazelix use, are safe and reversible, and do not require terminal-engine knowledge. Ratconfig capability is not a reason to promote a setting; it is the reason every remaining public setting can stay discoverable in All.

## Deliberate All-only groups

Renderer backends and filters, developer logging, bindings, hint rules, shell and editor commands, environment and working-directory overrides, detailed color entries, font slot and symbol-map internals, platform override tables, navigation internals, background images, and other specialist controls remain All-only by default. Non-inherited values from these groups still surface in Overview.

enable-scroll-bar leaves the current Overview set because managed Zellij makes terminal scrollbar chrome secondary. scrollback-history-limit remains All-only because managed Zellij owns ordinary workspace scrollback. Detailed colors remain All-only because mars.appearance.preset is the Overview appearance owner.

## Ownership and implementation

- Wait for mirror yazelix-5vl-f8d and pin the exact published Mars revision.
- Consume the schema-versioned Mars JSON artifact directly from that source revision without linking or retaining the full Mars runtime in the Mars-free package.
- Replace MARS_FIELDS as completeness owner. Keep only MARS_RECOMMENDED_PATHS and bounded Yazelix apply or display policy locally.
- Preserve every owner-advertised shape and choice. Do not silently narrow values such as Mars appearance auto; if a native choice conflicts with the Yazelix theme switcher, resolve that product interaction explicitly while keeping the owner value discoverable.
- Let Ratconfig render supported scalar and structured shapes generically. Safe values are editable; shapes that cannot be persisted safely remain read-only with an exact mars/config.toml action.
- Keep sparse user overrides, packaged-default inheritance, atomic writes, native validation, and Home Manager read-only ownership.
- Use conservative truthful apply labels for newly exposed fields; do not claim live behavior without evidence. The closed live-timing bead remains evidence for the original nine fields.
- Update configuration docs, architecture, changelog, and LOC scorecards for the visible inventory and Overview set.

## Verification

- The pinned Mars revision and inventory schema version are asserted at the consumption boundary.
- A focused owner-to-model parity check proves every public Mars catalog entry becomes one Ratconfig field and no local completeness list remains.
