- Behavioral checks prove representative Overview fields are visible, an inherited advanced field is hidden in Overview but present in All, explicit and externally managed advanced fields remain in Overview, and search finds advanced and platform-constrained entries.
- A focused contract proves window.blur is discoverable and accepts exactly false, true, macos-glass-regular, and macos-glass-clear while rejecting unknown strings.
- Representative scalar, enum, union, list, table, and structured entries preserve owner metadata and editability.
- Sparse write and reset checks prove unrelated native Mars values and package paths are not materialized; the reset action previews the Mars value and origin that will be inherited.
- A non-recommended Home Manager-managed value remains visible in Overview and read-only.
- cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzx-config/Cargo.toml, nix flake check, nix flake show --all-systems, nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs, nix build .#runtime --no-link --print-build-logs, and a temporary profile install exit 0.
- No GUI session is launched unless separately requested.

## Stop conditions

- Stop until Mars yzt-ehg is published and mirror yazelix-5vl-f8d records the exact revision and artifact path.
- Stop if consumption would retain the full Mars runtime in the Mars-free package.
- Stop if Ratconfig can accept a value rejected by the pinned Mars parser.
- Stop if implementation starts mirroring Mars paths, defaults, choices, descriptions, or platform constraints locally.
- Stop if Overview can hide any non-inherited non-recommended value.
- Stop if the work adds Mars-specific schema or rendering code to Ratconfig.

## Maintenance notes

Overview is Yazelix editorial metadata, not a second Mars schema. All follows the exact public owner catalog. Rejected alternatives: deriving completeness from packaged config.toml, copying the Mars Rust structs into Yazelix, promoting every generically editable setting to Overview, and exposing internal derived state as user configuration.

## Architecture constraint

Overview is a generic attention surface, not a second schema: it combines optional recommended identities with every explicit or invalid field. The configuration owner remains authoritative for paths, types, defaults, constraints, and writable semantics. Inline editing is allowed only when this integration has owner-backed validation and safe persistence; otherwise keep the value read-only and expose its exact native-file action. This child depends on the Ratconfig Overview integration in .13 unless it is already a completed focused slice.
notes:
Sparse-intent architecture for the decisive vertical slice:
- The exact Mars catalog is the only supported-inventory and semantic-metadata owner.
- Yazelix resolves baseline as the Mars value that would apply with the sparse user override removed, including the pinned Mars fallback and packaged defaults/mars/config.toml layer.
- The user TOML records intent; effective is the resolved merged value. An explicit value equal to baseline remains explicit and visible.
- Reset deletes the user path and recomputes baseline/effective state; it must not write the catalog or packaged default.
- Default TOML contributes observed packaged values only. It cannot discover window.blur or any other supported-but-absent setting.
- Ratconfig controls come from explicit capability mapping backed by the Mars parser and Yazelix's safe sparse patcher. Catalog type metadata may inform the host, but Ratconfig does not treat a type label as write authority.
- Unsupported unions, tables, or structured collections remain read-only with the exact Mars file action.
Success means complete owner-catalog discovery without a local MARS_FIELDS completeness list or per-field type/default/constraint mirrors. If that still requires substantial field-specific Yazelix logic, stop and revisit the adapter before other tools.

Invalid-input boundary: if the Mars owner parser localizes a bad field, keep that field visible with its diagnostic. If the sparse Mars TOML is wholly unparseable, expose a source-level diagnostic and exact mars/config.toml action; do not add a second tolerant parser merely to populate rows. Catalog acceptance proves owner-level validity, not suitability for the current runtime environment.

title: Expose all cursor settings and review Overview recommendations
description:

title: Keep Zellij configuration discoverable without mirroring its schema
description:
## Why

Zellij has a broad native KDL surface, while Yazelix safely edits only a curated scalar sidecar and reserves several top-level nodes for integration policy. A version-locked local inventory of every Zellij setting would duplicate Zellij's schema, grow with upstream internals, and still be unable to edit structured values safely.

## User-visible contract

Overview contains the high-value curated Zellij fields. All contains every field that Yazelix deliberately types and edits. The Zellij tab also exposes the native config file action, integration-owned nodes with an exact ownership explanation, and preserved opaque top-level scalars as unvalidated native entries. Search finds the curated fields and these truthful discovery surfaces. Yazelix never implies that its curated list is the complete native Zellij schema.

## Ownership

- Zellij owns native names, types, accepted values, and semantic validity.
- Yazelix owns its curated editable subset, integration guards, native-file route, and Overview recommendation policy.
- Ratconfig owns generic Overview/All, search, file actions, and diagnostic rendering; it does not learn KDL or Zellij policy.

## Scope

In scope:
- Review the current curated Zellij fields, including theme, and decide which belong in Overview.
- Keep safe known scalars on the existing sparse sidecar path.
- Make the native file action and integration ownership guidance useful and searchable.
- Present safely preserved unknown top-level scalars honestly as opaque/unvalidated rather than inventing field schemas for them.
- Verify guarded nodes remain non-editable and name their Yazelix owner.
- Document this partial-schema boundary in current-state configuration and architecture docs.
- Count this surface as complete when every Yazelix-owned curated field is classified and every other native shape has an honest owner/action or blocking diagnostic.

Out of scope:
- No complete local Zellij inventory, copied schema, scraped documentation catalog, or literal mirror test.
- No generic KDL editor/parser in Ratconfig.
- No relaxation of keybind, shell, layout, plugin, environment, session, or attachment ownership.
- No claim that every native value applies live.
- No Zellij fork change unless a separate user decision chooses an upstream machine-readable schema contract.

## Verification

A focused contract test proves: a curated Overview field; a curated All-only field if review selects one; search across those fields; a safe opaque scalar visible as unvalidated and preserved; a guarded node visible with ownership guidance but not editable; and the native file action. Existing sidecar guard parity, sparse write, reset, active-file patch, and scoped-diagnostic tests remain green. Run yzx-config tests and nix flake check.

## Stop conditions

Stop if completion requires manually enumerating the native Zellij schema. Stop if discovery is achieved by making guarded nodes writable. Stop if an opaque or structured value is labeled valid without Zellij-owned evidence. Stop if runtime and UI guards diverge.

## Rejected alternatives

Rejected: a full Yazelix Zellij schema, a Ratconfig KDL adapter, documentation scraping, and hiding all unexposed native state. Revisit complete field-by-field native discovery only if the pinned Zellij fork publishes a stable machine-readable catalog that Yazelix can consume without becoming its owner.


## Architecture constraint

Overview is a generic attention surface, not a second schema: it combines optional recommended identities with every non-inherited field, including explicit, invalid, and externally managed state. The configuration owner remains authoritative for paths, types, defaults, constraints, and writable semantics. Inline editing is allowed only when this integration has owner-backed validation and safe persistence; otherwise keep the value read-only and expose its exact native-file action. This child depends on the Ratconfig Overview integration in .13 unless it is already a completed focused slice.
notes: This bead no longer promises a complete field mirror for Zellij. Completion is ownership-based: curated typed fields are classified, native configuration remains reachable, guarded nodes explain their owner, and safely preserved opaque scalars are rendered truthfully. yazelix-5vl-hkk owns the prerequisite preservation/diagnostic boundary; the theme bead remains a valid curated field because its choices are checked directly against assets embedded by the pinned fork.

title: Expose all Yazi settings and review Overview recommendations
description:

- crates/yzx-config/src/file_actions.rs:124-165 exposes yazi.toml, init.lua, keymap.toml, package.toml, and theme.toml.

## User-Visible Contract

All exposes every setting known to the packaged Yazi version for finite native TOML surfaces. Overview contains the high-value file-manager and appearance settings selected by review. Existing user values, installed flavors, complex arrays/tables, keymaps, package metadata, and Lua remain discoverable with the correct structured or file-action treatment. Search can find known settings that are absent from both packaged and user TOML.

## Scope

In scope:
- Reconcile the generic TOML rows with a complete version-locked Yazi inventory.
- Review every finite non-recommended yazi.toml and theme.toml setting for an Overview recommendation.
- Keep complex values read-only, preserve file actions, and retain installed flavor choices.
- Classify keymap.toml, package.toml, and init.lua honestly without pretending arbitrary keymaps, package ids, or Lua are finite scalar settings.
- Preserve materialization, managed opener and Git fetchers, plugin/flavor assets, Home Manager sources, and invalid-input failure behavior.

Out of scope:

- No duplication of Yazi validation or schema in local catalogs.

## Verification

- cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzx-config/Cargo.toml exits 0.
- cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzx-yazi-config/Cargo.toml exits 0.
- nix flake check exits 0, including yzx_yazi_materialization.
- Tests prove one absent known setting is searchable, one complex value is read-only, one flavor is selectable, and one explicit non-recommended override stays visible in Overview.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the inventory cannot be tied to packaged Yazi.
- Stop if complete discovery would require parsing arbitrary Lua or keymap commands as scalar settings.
- Stop if owner schema use weakens managed opener or Git fetcher restoration.
- Stop if work duplicates or reverses yazelix-5vl-fg2.

## Maintenance Notes

Dependency decision: build on Ratconfig arbitrary TOML document rendering and Yazi-owned metadata. Effort M/L, risk MED, confidence HIGH in the current partial-versus-complete distinction.

## Architecture constraint

Overview is a generic attention surface, not a second schema: it combines optional recommended identities with every non-inherited field, including explicit, invalid, and externally managed state. The configuration owner remains authoritative for paths, types, defaults, constraints, and writable semantics. Inline editing is allowed only when this integration has owner-backed validation and safe persistence; otherwise keep the value read-only and expose its exact native-file action. This child depends on the Ratconfig Overview integration in .13 unless it is already a completed focused slice.

title: Review Nushell configuration for Overview and All
description:
## Why

Nushell env.nu and config.nu are executable source files rather than a finite settings document. A complete-discovery promise must state this boundary honestly while still reviewing the finite Yazelix-owned shell settings that influence managed Nu. Treating arbitrary Nu code as inferred settings would be unsafe and misleading.

## Evidence

- crates/yzx-config/src/file_actions.rs:106-120 exposes nu/env.nu and nu/config.nu only as native file actions.
- runtime/yzx-nu.rs layers packaged Nu, optional host mise activation, and user Nu source.
- defaults/nu owns packaged startup code.
- defaults/config.toml shell.program selects the managed shell while Starship has a separate sparse TOML surface.

## User-Visible Contract

All exposes every finite Yazelix-owned setting that controls managed Nushell and keeps both Nu source files visibly accessible. Ratconfig does not claim that arbitrary Nu language code is a finite settings schema. Overview contains the shell controls selected by review. Search for Nushell concepts reaches the owning semantic field or native file action.

## Scope

In scope:
- Inventory finite Yazelix-owned Nu integration controls across root config, runtime inputs, Starship handoff, mise behavior, and native file actions.
- Review all finite non-recommended controls for an Overview recommendation.
- Decide whether any current fixed integration behavior deserves an explicit setting; record rejected candidates without creating them automatically.
- Make native Nu files and their apply order discoverable in Overview/All UI.
- Preserve packaged-first layering, optional mise behavior, Starship separation, Home Manager ownership, and executable-source semantics.
- Add search and ownership tests.

Out of scope:
- No parser or structured editor for Nushell code.
- No general environment-variable browser, shell module system, or new setting without a separate user-approved slice.
- No merging of Starship or shell-program ownership into Nu files.

## Verification

- Focused yzx-config tests prove all finite Nu integration controls and both native file actions are discoverable.
- nix flake check exits 0 and managed Nu contract checks preserve source order.
- A search test reaches a non-recommended Nu-related owner without parsing Nu source.
- Current docs explain the finite-schema boundary plainly.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the implementation starts interpreting arbitrary Nu code.
- Stop if the pass invents a semantic option merely to make a fixed behavior appear in All.
- Stop if Starship or shell-program ownership becomes duplicated.

## Maintenance Notes

This pass may legitimately conclude that the two Nu files remain file actions rather than settings rows. That is complete and honest for an executable configuration language. Effort S/M, risk LOW, confidence HIGH.

## Architecture constraint

Overview is a generic attention surface, not a second schema: it combines optional recommended identities with every non-inherited field, including explicit, invalid, and externally managed state. The configuration owner remains authoritative for paths, types, defaults, constraints, and writable semantics. Inline editing is allowed only when this integration has owner-backed validation and safe persistence; otherwise keep the value read-only and expose its exact native-file action. This child depends on the Ratconfig Overview integration in .13 unless it is already a completed focused slice.

title: Keep config popup state across toggles
description:
## Why

Closing and reopening the config popup restarts Ratconfig on its first tab, making repeated configuration work unnecessarily tedious. Toggling the popup within the same workspace should resume where the user left it.

## Evidence

- Runtime dogfooding report on 2026-07-14: toggling the config popup returns to the first tab.
- `defaults/zellij/config.kdl:16-22` defines `config_popup` without `toggle_close_behavior`, so the popup plugin uses its default close policy.
- `../yazelix-zellij-popup/src/popup_contract.rs:868-872` supports `toggle_close_behavior "hide"`; `src/main.rs:380-389` suppresses the pane without terminating its process.
- `../ratconfig/src/editor.rs:98-105` initializes a new app at tab and row zero. Avoiding process restart therefore preserves the existing in-memory UI state without adding another persistence owner.
- The agent popup already uses the same keep-alive policy in `defaults/zellij/config.kdl:24-31`.

## Scope

### In scope

- Give the packaged config popup the existing hide-on-toggle policy.
- Within the same live tab/workspace and process, reopening the popup preserves at least the selected config tab and selected row.
- Keep popup restart-on-real-workspace-CWD-change behavior intact.
- Update the focused generated-config contract, CHANGELOG, and README LOC scorecard as required.

### Out of scope

- Disk persistence across Yazelix sessions, Ratconfig restarts, crashes, upgrades, or workspace-CWD changes.
- New Ratconfig state files, a generic popup-plugin behavior change, or making every popup stateful.
- Persisting unsaved configuration values beyond Ratconfig's existing process behavior.

## Implementation Notes

1. Set `toggle_close_behavior "hide"` only on the main-owned `config` popup specification.
2. Update the existing popup-spec contract in `checks/yzx-contracts.rs` to prove the generated KDL carries that policy.
3. Do not add Ratconfig serialization or modify the popup child unless the packaged policy demonstrably fails.
4. Treat the hidden process's existing in-memory state as the sole state owner.

## Verification

- The focused Yazelix contract expects hide behavior for config and preserves the existing policies for agent, git, and menu.
- In a live Nova session, navigate away from the first config tab and row, toggle the popup off, then toggle it on; the same tab and row return.
- Changing to a genuinely different workspace CWD retains the popup plugin's existing stale-process restart behavior.
- `nix flake check`, `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs`, and a temporary-profile install pass succeed.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Zellij terminates or recreates the pane despite the existing hide policy; capture pane/process identity before changing Ratconfig.
- Stop if preserving state requires a disk format or cross-session lifecycle decision; that is separate user scope.
- Stop before changing generic popup defaults or other popup lifecycles.

## Maintenance Notes

Owner: the main-repository generated popup specification.

Effort: XS. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.

Rejected alternatives: a Ratconfig state sidecar, restoring tabs from the Yazelix config TOML, changing the generic popup default, and keeping every popup alive.
close_reason: Config popup now uses the existing hide-on-toggle lifecycle, preserving Ratconfig tab and row state while its process remains alive. Generated-config regression coverage, current-state docs, changelog, LOC scorecard, flake checks, package build, temporary profile install, and installed runtime upgrade all pass; no Ratconfig or popup-child changes were needed. GUI dogfood remains for the user because agents do not launch GUI sessions implicitly.

title: Follow successful managed Yazi opens to the target directory
description:
## Why

The standard managed Yazi file opener invokes `yzx-open` but leaves the sidebar at its previous cwd. This is visible when a file is opened from search, flat view, or another result whose containing directory differs from the current Yazi directory. The existing Yazi `Alt z` plugin demonstrates the intended navigation: it emits `cd` to the chosen directory before opening it in the editor.

This is local sidebar navigation, not a tab-workspace retarget. The canonical workspace root and hidden-agent lifecycle must remain governed by `yazelix-5vl-f69`.

## User-visible contract

- After a successful open originating from the managed Yazi sidebar, that same Yazi instance follows the primary target: a file moves it to the file's containing directory; a directory moves it to that directory.
- The opened absolute target still reaches the managed editor unchanged.
- Following the target does not change the canonical tab workspace, rename the workspace from the target, restart the hidden agent, or move existing shell panes.
- A failed or rejected editor handoff leaves Yazi at its previous cwd and reports the existing clear error.
- Opens not originating from the managed sidebar do not move it.
- Existing `Alt z` behavior remains consistent with this contract and should reuse the same smallest reliable target-follow mechanism when practical.

## Scope

- Identify the originating managed Yazi instance reliably even when `yzx-open` focuses Helix before the open completes. Prefer the request's Yazi identity or the orchestrator's registered sidebar identity over the currently focused pane.
- Emit one targeted Yazi `cd` only after the managed open is known to have succeeded.
- For multiple targets, follow the containing directory of the primary/first target while passing every requested target to the editor.
- Preserve paths containing spaces and non-UTF-8-safe process arguments to the extent supported by the existing opener contract.
- Cover bridge reuse and new-editor fallback, plus files reached from nested search/flat-view results.
- Keep sidebar state publication and Starship/Git refresh behavior flowing through the existing Yazi `cd` subscription.

## Guardrails

- Do not treat Yazi cwd as the canonical tab workspace or feed this local move back into explicit retargeting.
- Do not broadcast `cd` to every Yazi instance or infer the recipient from whichever pane is focused after Helix opens.
- Do not change cwd before success unless the operation can reliably roll back.
- Do not add a second target parser, Git-root resolver, or workspace-state store.
- Do not fold global `Alt z`, hide-on-open policy, or general sidebar reveal behavior into this bug.
- Keep this in the main repo by default: use the existing managed opener, upstream Yazi `emit-to`/`cd`, and orchestrator sidebar registration. Do not change the Helix fork, archived Yazi fork, or popup child for this local navigation effect.

## Verification

Use a focused matrix covering a file already in the current directory, a nested file selected from search/flat view, a directory target, multiple files, a path with spaces, bridge success, new-editor fallback success, rejected open, and no registered sidebar. Observe both the sidebar cwd and the unchanged orchestrator workspace/agent identity.

## Maintenance notes

P2 main-repo runtime bug. Depends on the canonical workspace contract so local target-follow cannot accidentally become another workspace-retarget path. No Helix fork change is expected.
acceptance_criteria: A successful managed-Yazi open moves only the originating sidebar Yazi to the primary file's containing directory or the opened directory. Nested/search results, multiple targets, paths with spaces, live-bridge reuse, and new-editor fallback are covered. Failed/rejected opens and non-Yazi invocations leave the sidebar cwd unchanged. The canonical orchestrator workspace, tab/workspace label, existing shell panes, and hidden-agent PID remain unchanged by this local navigation, and the implementation reuses existing sidebar registration and Yazi cd publication rather than adding state. The fix remains in main-repo integration code and uses existing child APIs; any child-repo change requires a focused demonstrated capability gap rather than convenience.
close_reason: Implemented targeted post-success Yazi follow in yzx-open; verified bridge reuse, new-editor fallback, file/directory targets, multiple targets, spaces, non-originating calls, failure behavior, runtime contracts, full flake checks, package build, and profile install.

title: Evaluate an editor-free workspace retarget command
description:
## Question

Should Yazelix expose a command or option that changes the active tab's canonical workspace without opening or focusing Helix?

The canonical workspace contract will make the pane orchestrator the per-tab root owner. That creates a useful capability independent of editor lifecycle, but the user-facing need, command name, discoverability, and side effects are not chosen yet.

## Evaluate

- A direct command such as `yzx workspace <path>`, a menu action, or a picker result that performs only an explicit tab retarget.
- Behavior when no editor exists, when an editor exists but has local cwd drift, and when the target is Git, non-Git, a nested repository, missing, or unreadable.
- Which consumers update: orchestrator root, tab/workspace label, status, future workspace-root panes, and hidden agent stale state.
- Which consumers stay local: existing shell panes, Yazi navigation, file-local terminals, and open editor buffers.
- Whether the sidebar should navigate to the new root or remain where it is.
- Relationship to the tab-level Alt-z picker: separate command, picker mode, or rejected overlap.
- Whether the action is valuable enough to justify a new public command/config surface.

## Guardrails

- This is a decision bead, not authorization to add a command, setting, or picker mode.
- Reuse the explicit-retarget primitive; do not create another workspace owner or target resolver.
- Do not `cd` every existing pane or implicitly create an editor.
- Preserve clear failures and cancellation without partial workspace state.

## Outcome

Record one maintainer decision with the chosen user-visible contract and rejected alternatives. If approved, create the smallest implementation bead after the command/key/menu surface is explicitly chosen. If rejected, close this bead with the reason and keep retargeting behind existing workflows.
acceptance_criteria: The bead closes with an explicit accept/reject decision. An accepted design names the invocation surface, target/root policy, editor/sidebar/agent effects, failure behavior, and relationship to Alt-z, and identifies the existing retarget owner it reuses. No runtime surface is added by this decision bead.

title: Keep the tab workspace stable across Yazi opens and agent toggles
description:
## Why

Opening a file or directory through the managed Yazi bridge can currently change the effective workspace based on the target path or the focused pane. That is too unstable for a tab-level workspace contract and can cause the hidden agent to be treated as stale even when the user only navigated within the same project.

The reported ignored-file path `/home/lucca/pjs/science/tree-seg-unsuper/docs/text/personal_files/transcription.md` is a useful regression case. Direct Git lookup and the installed `yzx-open` resolve the parent worktree correctly, but live inspection showed Yazi at `.../personal_files` while Helix and the agent remained at the worktree root. Popup fallback then selected the focused Yazi pane cwd. The core defect is therefore divergent workspace ownership and focus-derived fallback, not ignored-file Git discovery alone.

Target-derived retargeting is also harmful outside that example: an ordinary open can enter a nested repository/submodule, and every non-Git file or directory currently promotes its own parent/path as the workspace.

## Existing source of truth

The locked pane-orchestrator child already owns versioned per-tab workspace state through `workspace_state_by_tab`, including bootstrap versus explicit provenance, `get_active_tab_session_state`, `retarget_workspace`, and `open_workspace_terminal`. The bar and screen consume that state. `yzx-open` currently bypasses it, while popup fallback and the agent derive cwd elsewhere.

Keep the pane orchestrator as the sole canonical owner. Main-repo `yzx-open` owns operation intent and sequencing: requested targets, ordinary open versus explicit retarget, Git/non-Git root policy, Helix bridge calls, and rollback/error reporting. It does not own another workspace-state map. Yazi cwd and ordinary pane cwd remain local navigation state.

The existing Helix fork bridge already exposes the required editor-local vocabulary: `helix.get_context`, `helix.set_cwd`, `helix.open_directory`, and `helix.open_files`. Reuse those actions. Avoid expanding the Helix fork; a new action, schema field, or editor-side policy is justified only by a focused failing contract showing that the current bridge cannot satisfy the user-visible behavior and that solving it outside Helix would require unsafe terminal keystrokes or duplicate editor state.

## User-visible contract

- Each managed tab has one canonical workspace root owned by the pane orchestrator.
- Before an explicit root exists, the first managed open promotes bootstrap state to an explicit root: the containing Git worktree when present, otherwise the opened directory or the file's parent.
- Once explicit, an ordinary file or directory open preserves that root, including ignored files, nested repositories/submodules, and non-Git descendants. The requested absolute file or directory still reaches Helix/picker behavior unchanged.
- An explicit workspace-retarget action is the only managed operation that changes the root. The current Yazi `Alt z` path must use this internal primitive until its separate tab-level picker bead replaces the UX.
- A successful retarget updates canonical state and the managed editor cwd as one operation. A failed or cancelled retarget leaves both unchanged.
- The hidden agent is requested with the canonical root. It survives navigation, pane focus changes, ordinary opens, and local editor cwd drift; it restarts only after a real canonical-root change.
- Raw Helix `:cd`, shell `cd`, and Yazi navigation may drift locally and do not redefine the tab workspace. The next managed open resynchronizes Helix to the canonical root without moving existing shells. A successful request originating in managed Yazi may still move that Yazi locally to the opened directory or file parent; this never changes the canonical root.
- On orchestrator restart, a pre-existing managed editor cwd may be used once as recovery evidence only when no explicit workspace state exists. It cannot continuously override explicit orchestrator state.
- Popup actions use the canonical root for workspace-scoped operations and an explicitly supplied request cwd for target-local operations. Focused-pane cwd is never a silent workspace fallback.

## Scope

- Route live bridge opens through the orchestrator's current per-tab session state and retarget action.
- Distinguish ordinary open from explicit retarget in the smallest request contract that cannot be confused by target type or focus.
- Promote bootstrap state exactly once and publish it to existing consumers.
- Route agent launch/staleness through orchestrator state rather than editor title, focused pane, or popup fallback.
- Make popup cwd choice request-specific: canonical workspace for workspace actions, explicit target cwd for target actions.
- Preserve requested absolute targets and clear errors for missing, unreadable, or rejected paths.
- Add focused regression coverage and enough diagnostics to identify tab id, root provenance, requested target, operation kind, and agent restart reason without creating another state store.
- Change the pane-orchestrator child only for canonical state/read-mutation primitives that belong to it, and the popup child only for generic explicit-request-cwd and hidden-reuse correctness if required. Neither child owns Git discovery, Yazelix workspace policy, or editor sequencing.
- Keep root policy, request coordination, Yazi integration, generated keymaps/layouts, packaging, documentation, and end-to-end checks in the main repo.
- Reuse the existing Helix bridge without changing the Helix fork unless a focused failing contract proves a missing editor-local primitive.
- Publish any required child commit before updating main-repo lock/release references, changelog, README LOC scorecard, and packaged runtime.

## Out of scope

- The speculative tab-level zoxide picker, editor-free retarget command, and `Alt m` pane policy are separate beads.
- The local managed-Yazi target-follow implementation is tracked separately; this foundation only guarantees that such navigation cannot retarget the tab workspace or restart the agent.
- General popup-framework redesign, layout expansion, Home Manager scope, or legacy compatibility.
- Automatically promoting arbitrary Helix `:cd` or pane cwd drift into workspace state.
- Replacing Helix or adding a second workspace owner.

## Implementation notes

- Main-repo `yzx-open` is the operation coordinator. Query current tab session state before deciding whether an open bootstraps, preserves, or explicitly retargets.
- Keep orchestrator mutation and existing Helix bridge calls transactional from the caller's perspective; if editor coordination fails, retain or restore the previous canonical root and return a clear error.
- Keep the orchestrator API state-oriented. Do not make it resolve Git roots or send `:cd`/`:open` text into Helix.
- If the popup child changes, keep the capability generic: compare hidden popup state with the caller's effective requested cwd. Workspace and agent policy remain outside the popup plugin.
- Derive tab/workspace labels from the canonical state already published by the orchestrator.
- The agent toggle must compare its stored launch root with canonical orchestrator state, not whichever pane is focused when the popup request arrives.
- Recovery precedence is explicit orchestrator state, then one-time managed-editor evidence, then existing bootstrap/default behavior.
- Prefer one contract-level test matrix over mirror tests for each literal/config value.

## Rejected alternatives

- Remove agent reset on cwd change: reduces visible harm but permits an agent from the old workspace to masquerade as current.
- Retarget on every target: makes file opening unexpectedly redefine the tab, especially in non-Git trees and nested repositories.
- Treat Helix cwd as the continuous source of truth: raw editor drift would silently restart agents and move other workspace-bound actions.
- Treat focused pane or Yazi cwd as canonical: navigation and focus become global state mutations.
- Freeze the initial root forever: prevents deliberate workspace switching and future tab-level picker workflows.
- Move workspace coordination into the Helix fork: increases fork delta, makes editor-local cwd a product owner, and couples non-editor workflows to Helix.

## Maintenance notes

P0 beta blocker, large integration slice. The bug spans main-repo coordination, the pane-orchestrator child, popup cwd semantics, the existing Helix bridge, and agent lifecycle. The desired result is fewer owners and no Helix fork expansion unless the current bridge is demonstrably insufficient.
acceptance_criteria:
- A normal Git worktree, the ignored `docs/text/personal_files/transcription.md` path, a nested repository/submodule, and a non-Git tree are covered for nested file and directory targets.
- With an explicit workspace, every ordinary live-bridge open preserves the orchestrator root while retaining the requested absolute file path or picker root.
- With no live editor, the first open establishes and publishes the correct Git or non-Git root and launches the editor with that cwd.
- Explicit retarget changes both orchestrator state and Helix cwd; ordinary open inside a nested repository does not.
- With Yazi focused and navigated below the workspace, toggling the hidden agent immediately before or after an ordinary open reveals the same PID at the canonical root.
- After an explicit retarget, the next agent reveal replaces the stale PID and launches at the new canonical root.
- A drift matrix with orchestrator=/repo, Helix=/repo/docs, Yazi=/repo/docs/text, and agent=/repo proves that a Yazi-originated open of `/repo/docs/text/personal_files/transcription.md` preserves the canonical root and agent PID while allowing the separate target-follow behavior to move Yazi locally to `/repo/docs/text/personal_files`.
- Orchestrator restart recovery may adopt a pre-existing managed editor cwd only when explicit workspace state is absent; later editor drift cannot overwrite explicit state.
- Orchestrator session state, Helix bridge context, workspace terminal cwd, and tab/workspace label agree after bootstrap and retarget.
- The current Yazi Alt-z path still performs a deliberate retarget through the new primitive pending its separate tab-level UX bead.
- The existing Helix bridge actions satisfy the contract without Helix fork source changes. If a focused failing test proves that impossible, the bead records the evidence, rejected main/orchestrator alternatives, and the smallest editor-local fork delta before implementation.
- Focused Rust/child checks, child WASI/Nix builds only for changed children, `nix flake check`, `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs`, and a temporary-profile install pass succeed.
notes: Live acceptance on 2026-07-15 used a disposable installed-runtime session. Bootstrap promoted the repo root, an ordinary nested-file bridge open resynchronized a deliberately drifted Helix cwd, and the hidden agent retained pane 1 / PID 2592505. Retargeting to yazelix-zellij-popup changed orchestrator and Helix roots, then replaced the stale agent with pane 3 / PID 2597519 at the new root. Direct MessageToPlugin URL/alias routing was rejected after dogfooding showed that Zellij's caller configuration could resolve a different plugin instance; the orchestrator instead finds the loaded yzpp instance in its existing pane manifest and messages its plugin ID.
close_reason: Completed and live-dogfooded

title: Track the published Mars config inventory
description:
## Purpose

Mirror Mars bead yzt-ehg, which owns the complete versioned public Mars settings inventory required by Yazelix config discovery.

## Contract

This mirror closes only after Mars publishes and pushes one exact revision containing a deterministic, schema-versioned JSON catalog at a stable source path. The catalog covers every public base setting and Linux, macOS, and Windows override; represents structured collections with truthful nested shapes; supplies defaults, finite choices, descriptions, and platform constraints; and explicitly excludes internal or derived state.

Mars parser-backed checks must prove advertised defaults, choices, unions, and representative structured values. window.blur must advertise exactly false, true, macos-glass-regular, and macos-glass-clear. The artifact must be consumable from the exact Mars source revision without linking or retaining the Mars terminal runtime.

## Ownership

- Mars owns inventory completeness and native setting semantics.
- Yazelix owns the optional 16-setting Overview recommendation list plus Yazelix-specific apply and display policy. The recommendations are presentation metadata, not a semantic schema; explicit and invalid settings appear in Overview automatically.
- Ratconfig remains generic.

Do not implement Mars completeness, copy Mars structs, scrape documentation, infer the catalog from packaged config.toml, or add a compatibility adapter in this mirror.

## Handoff

Record the exact published Mars revision and stable artifact path, update the Yazelix Mars pin without disturbing unrelated lock inputs, then close this mirror. Consumer bead yazelix-5vl-core-all-config-discovery-lsw.3 remains blocked until that publication and owns actual Ratconfig integration.

## Architecture handoff

The consumer uses the owner catalog as the only completeness and semantic source. Inline editing is exposed only for catalog shapes that the Mars parser can validate and the Yazelix integration can persist safely. Unsupported structured values remain read-only with the native Mars file action. Mirror .13 supplies the generic Ratconfig Overview contract; no CLI work is part of this handoff.

title: Package official Yazi flavors and render native Yazi config
description: Provide one Yazi Ratconfig tab that renders the user yazi.toml override against Nova packaged defaults, renders native theme.toml with dynamic dark/light flavor choices, and retains file actions for complex native structures. Package the five flavors physically hosted by yazi-rs/flavors at a Yazi-compatible revision. Ratconfig remains generic; yzx-config owns paths, flavor discovery, Home Manager ownership, validation, and atomic native writes; yzx-yazi-config remains the runtime materializer. Rejected: Classic catalog snapshots, a second semantic theme setting, a static flavor registry, vendoring Yazi internal defaults, random-theme machinery, and a generic create-arbitrary-key form.
acceptance_criteria: Nova packages Catppuccin Latte, Frappé, Macchiato, Mocha, and Dracula from an exact official yazi-rs/flavors revision without preview assets. Ratconfig has one Yazi tab where safe yazi.toml and theme.toml values render from their native files, dark/light flavor rows offer the packaged and user-installed flavors discovered from actual directories, complex structures remain read-only, and every native Yazi file remains openable. Writes preserve comments and unrelated keys, reset removes the user override, Home Manager-owned files are read-only with exact option guidance, and changes apply on the next Yazi launch. Focused Rust contracts, Yazi debug validation, Nix checks, temporary profile installation, documentation, changelog, and LOC scorecard pass.
notes: Fresh-eyes and simplification pass 2026-07-14: fixed two host-composition bugs. The Yazi tab no longer mixes Ratconfig's six-column TOML table with three-column file-action rows; it uses one consistent settings layout. The Advanced tab again uses Ratconfig's reserved advanced identity, restoring hidden diagnostics and status columns. Centralized Yazi source routing/ownership, deduplicated optional text loading, and reduced test scaffolding. Net first pass: 20 fewer Rust lines and 17 fewer tracked lines after the changelog note. A second independent pass rejected an ambiguous flavor package named only .yazi: it previously appeared as an empty picker choice and was accepted as installed. The end-to-end contract covers that boundary; production LOC stayed flat and the regression adds one test line. A third failure-path pass fixed dangling managed file/asset symlinks being silently treated as absent and ensured that write, replacement, or rename errors discard the staged runtime. A fourth boundary pass extended that protection to a dangling managed Yazi root, made the runtime ignore empty .yazi asset names consistently with the picker, and rejected incomplete user flavor overrides before they can replace valid packaged flavors. All failures were reproduced before their fixes and folded into the existing transaction and asset contracts. Rejected a Ratconfig cross-repo header/API change: row-specific source metadata remains a generic Ratconfig limitation, while row ownership, write rejection, and file-action details are correct; revisit only if misleading header metadata proves user-significant. Also rejected an intermediate field-container refactor because rustfmt made it larger without reducing ownership or branching, sharing the tiny symlink-aware existence helper across crates because that would add a dependency for no conceptual reduction, and materializer locking/platform-specific rename because the observed concurrent paths preserve a completed runtime and do not justify a new ownership layer. Fifth fresh-eyes pass reproduced broken runtime symlinks when the materializer received relative config or state paths; all three inputs are now resolved once at the boundary, and the existing asset contract runs on relative paths. Simplification collapsed two competing tab-level Yazi sources into one directory source while preserving exact yazi-config and yazi-theme field routing, so Ratconfig no longer chooses an arbitrary file for the multi-file tab header. Rejected post-processing synthetic empty flavor defaults because it would misstate Yazi's absence-based default and needs a generic Ratconfig reset contract before it can be represented honestly. Sixth fresh-eyes pass reproduced a destructive override alias: when the managed Yazi config was equal to or nested under generated state/yazi, replacement deleted the source config. The materializer now canonicalizes the state root and rejects both lexical and symlink-resolved config overlap before staging; the transaction contract proves the source yazi.toml survives. Kept the guard at the actual replacement boundary rather than banning all nearby config/state layouts or adding a second caller-side policy. Seventh fresh-eyes pass reproduced a descendant alias missed by the root guard: a managed passthrough file or asset symlink resolving into generated state/yazi was staged, then its target was deleted during replacement, leaving broken runtime links. One shared replacement-boundary check now protects the config root and every user-linked file, plugin, and flavor before the old runtime is touched; the transaction test proves the old source survives. Simplification removed test-only embedding and copying of the production Yazi default from yzx-config and its Nix source preparation; representative TOML remains in the focused test while the flake integration checks continue to exercise the actual packaged default. Rejected scanning and canonicalizing every managed input up front because copied TOML and Lua inputs are safe after staging, and duplicate traversal would add policy and change errors. Eighth fresh-eyes pass reproduced a semantic repair failure: native theme.toml with a numeric or boolean flavor value rendered that row as a free-form non-string editor instead of the installed-flavor picker. The Yazi owner now keeps dark and light rows typed as string choices while Ratconfig still owns dotted-path patchability; the end-to-end test starts from dark = 42 and repairs it to an installed custom flavor without losing comments or unrelated keys. Simplification hoisted the two identical yzx-yazi-config derivations into one private flake helper, removing three Nix lines and one duplicate owner without changing public outputs. Rejected forcing generic StructuredOnly rows editable because non-table parent paths cannot be patched safely, and rejected exposing the materializer through package passthru solely for check reuse because that would create a public surface.
close_reason: Implemented and verified the official five-flavor package, native Ratconfig Yazi tab, dynamic flavor discovery, safe writes/resets, Home Manager ownership, and runtime materialization.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Structured-value follow-up chosen 2026-07-14: Ratconfig owns compact complete previews for generic TOML tables and complex arrays. Normal-mode e on a structured row reuses OpenFile only when exactly one file action shares the field source_id; Yazelix assigns distinct yazi-config and yazi-theme owners so the shortcut cannot guess between files. Rejected an expandable tree and in-TUI structured editor because they add navigation, state, validation, and patch policy; full editing remains file-driven.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Second structured-value audit 2026-07-14: non-writable native file actions are now disabled in the model from the existing mutation guard, so Ratconfig shows unavailable and exact Home Manager or read-only guidance before emitting an open intent; activation still rechecks to cover permission and symlink races. Generic TOML previews now preserve infinities and NaN through a compact TOML fallback, and direct non-finite floats stay structured/read-only because the JSON edit contract cannot represent them. Simplification deleted a redundant renderer composition test and its orphan helper; Ratconfig retains separate model and custom-table renderer contracts. Rejected removing the backend mutation recheck because model state can become stale, and rejected a bespoke recursive preview serializer because the TOML serializer covers the only JSON gap with less code.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Third structured-value audit 2026-07-14: reproduced unsafe vacuous type inference for empty TOML arrays. The schema-free helper treated [] as a string list and advertised string-only editing despite having no element-type evidence. Empty arrays now remain complete structured/read-only values; only non-empty all-string arrays infer the editable string-list path. Schema-owned lists such as agent.args are unchanged. Rejected inferring empty-array type from the default document because that would add cross-document typing policy to the generic helper; exact native file editing already covers the ambiguous case.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Post-commit integration audit 2026-07-14: local Cargo checks passed because yzx-config consumes the sibling Ratconfig checkout, but nix flake check correctly failed against flake.lock's published Ratconfig 80c9329: the Yazi contract received the old count-only value '[3 items]' instead of '[1,4,0]'. The feature is not installable until Ratconfig 36375ca is published and Yazelix locks that exact revision. Rejected weakening the contract, vendoring Ratconfig, or committing a local-path flake input; the existing remote pin remains the single canonical dependency owner.

title: Consume scoped diagnostics without mirroring Zellij
description:
## Why

Ratconfig now routes diagnostics by all fields, one source, or one exact field. Yazelix still needs an honest policy for native Zellij sidecars whose schema is larger and faster-moving than the curated settings UI. Mirroring that schema in Yazelix would create a second owner and turn every new native scalar into a host release.

## User-visible contract

Known Yazelix-exposed Zellij fields remain typed and editable. A top-level native leaf node outside the managed and guarded token sets is preserved verbatim, remains visible as an unvalidated native entry, and does not invalidate unrelated fields. Yazelix does not claim that Zellij will accept that opaque entry. Malformed documents, unknown blocks, and integration-owned nodes block writes at source scope. Invalid known fields block only their own field and remain repairable.

## Ownership

- Ratconfig owns generic diagnostic scopes and rendering.
- Yazelix owns the guarded sidecar boundary, curated editable fields, sparse patch/reset behavior, and classification of document shapes it can preserve safely.
- Zellij owns native setting names, types, values, and semantic validation.

## Scope

In scope:
- Consume Ratconfig scoped diagnostics at the exact published revision already pinned for this work.
- Route known invalid values to exact field scope and unsafe document state to Zellij source scope.
- Treat unknown top-level leaf nodes as opaque nonblocking entries and preserve their original text across unrelated saves and resets. Do not interpret their arguments, properties, or values.
- Keep unknown blocks, malformed structure, duplicate/ambiguous known nodes, and integration-owned nodes source-blocking.
- Delete the local default_mode value inventory, its mode-specific parser branch, and the per-value Nix validation loop. default_mode is one regression example, not a special host-owned schema.
- Delete the sidecar only when reset leaves no meaningful native entries.
- Keep the native file action as the escape hatch for structured or unexposed Zellij configuration.
- Update current-state docs, CHANGELOG, and the README LOC scorecard for the resulting behavior.

Out of scope:
- No generic KDL adapter in Ratconfig.
- No complete Zellij schema, scraped catalog, compatibility shim, or Zellij fork change.
- No claim that an opaque entry is semantically valid or applies live.
- Complete Zellij discoverability and Core review remain in yazelix-5vl-core-all-config-discovery-lsw.5.

## Verification

Use a few contract tests: the reported default_mode normal case; a different future-looking leaf form; exact preservation across an unrelated known-field save and reset; a repairable invalid known field; a source blocker; an unknown block; and a second unaffected config source. Then run cargo fmt/check, strict Clippy, yzx-config tests, flake checks, package build, temporary profile install, and git diff --check.

## Stop conditions

Stop if the writer cannot identify and preserve an opaque leaf node without rewriting it. Stop if semantic Zellij validation is duplicated locally. Stop if narrowing diagnostics allows a guarded or structurally unsafe sidecar to be written.

## Rejected alternatives

Rejected: moving Zellij/KDL semantics into Ratconfig; maintaining a complete Yazelix Zellij schema; special-casing default_mode values; validating an enumerated mode list in Nix; treating unknown blocks as safe; and reducing the UI to only a native-file action. The selected boundary preserves the useful picker while keeping native semantics with Zellij.
notes:
Completed against Ratconfig commit f70bbfbc3e021fb3f51fea7399b020bc1ab91c49. Ratconfig owns generic diagnostic routing; Yazelix owns the sparse Zellij sidecar boundary and curated typed fields; Zellij owns the native schema and semantic validity. Unknown top-level leaf nodes outside managed, guarded, and colliding token sets are visible as nonblocking unvalidated diagnostics and remain unchanged across unrelated saves and resets. Invalid known scalar values affect only their exact field and remain repairable. Integration-owned nodes, unknown blocks, malformed structure, duplicate managed nodes, nested-token collisions, and multiple nodes encoded on one line remain source blockers.

The implementation has no default_mode allowlist, native value catalog, per-value Nix validation loop, generic Ratconfig KDL adapter, compatibility shim, or Zellij fork change. Reset removes the sidecar only when no meaningful native entries remain.

Fresh-eyes reviews fixed the handwritten-subset boundaries that could make the sparse writer target the wrong source text. Managed quoted strings no longer truncate at whitespace; quoted braces and comment-like text are not structure; unterminated quotes are source-blocking; quoted booleans and integers are not coerced; and same-line KDL separators or content after block closes are source-blocking. A single trailing semicolon remains supported.

The latest pass checked suspicious shapes with the packaged Zellij 0.44.3 parser. Zellij confirmed that slashdash can comment the next node across a newline, block comments can contain managed-looking lines, an unquoted backslash continues a node, and ui or pane_frames can carry extra arguments or properties. All of those shapes are native-file-only and source-block writes because a physical-line patch cannot preserve their semantics. Zellij also confirmed that managed strings require quotes and decode escapes. Yazelix accepts only quoted, escape-free managed strings; bare or escaped known strings receive exact-field diagnostics and remain repairable without poisoning unrelated settings.

The simplification leaves one lexical scan responsible for line comments, structural comments and continuations, quote balance, braces, semicolons, and safe-leaf classification. The parser no longer rescans ordinary lines after block-close handling. Exact managed block opening is centralized, scalar parsing stays pure with one diagnostic owner, and block state is a fixed depth rather than a collection of repeated names. The final scorecard is 18,970 lines: 131 above the 18,839 mode-specific prototype and 333 above the 18,637 clean baseline shared with the theme slice. This pass adds 50 lines for the confirmed structural boundaries, focused cases, and current-state documentation.

Focused contracts cover custom quoted theme names with spaces and braces, opaque quoted braces and comment-like text, typed repair, exact leaf preservation, source isolation, guarded nodes, duplicates, blocks, nested-token ambiguity, same-line separators, structural KDL comments, continuations, managed-block metadata, and bare or escaped strings. Rejected further deletion: typed scalar parsing is required for Yazelix-owned fields; lexical scanning is required to prove safe preservation; duplicate, collision, separator, comment, continuation, and block-shape guards protect sparse writes. Rejected a generic KDL parser, spawning Zellij for every UI read or write, and parsing multiple nodes per line because they add another semantic owner or runtime dependency; the native file remains the escape hatch for richer KDL.

Verification passed: direct Zellij setup checks for every newly guarded syntax family, cargo fmt check, all 45 yzx-config tests, strict Clippy, git diff check, nix flake check with 43 checks, nix flake show --all-systems, nix build .#yazelix, isolated profile installation, and installed runtime refresh. Live theme switching and reset dogfood remains owned by yazelix-5vl-core-all-config-discovery-lsw.12.
close_reason: Completed scoped diagnostics and hardened the sparse Zellij writer against native multiline and structured KDL forms.

title: Use a human fallback title for the agent pane
description:
## Why

Opening the managed agent popup can expose its full Nix store executable path as the native pane title, for example `/nix/store/...-yzx-agent/bin/yzx-agent`. That is implementation leakage and visually noisy. The stable Yazelix role is simply `agent`.

The current popup deliberately preserves terminal titles because provider spinner titles drive agent activity decorations. The fix must therefore supply a human fallback without pinning the pane title or suppressing meaningful provider updates.

## User-visible contract

When a managed agent pane has no meaningful provider-emitted terminal title, its frame shows `agent`, never a Nix store path or raw launcher command. Provider-emitted titles, including spinner titles, remain visible and continue driving tab activity. This holds for the automatic provider bootstrap and a custom `agent.command`.

## Evidence

- `defaults/zellij/config.kdl:26`: the managed popup has internal `pane_title "agent_popup"` and `preserve_terminal_title true`.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij-popup/src/main.rs:321`: preserving terminal titles currently renames the new pane to an empty string, allowing the command path to become the initial visible title.
- `runtime/yzx-agent.rs:31`: the automatic launcher execs the first available provider from the managed bootstrap.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator/src/ai_pane_activity_contract.rs:60`: spinner-shaped terminal titles are the activity signal.
- `README.md:122`: the published runtime contract says spinner titles drive compact tab activity without changing native tab names.
- Manual dogfooding on 2026-07-16 showed `/nix/store/...-yzx-agent/bin/yzx-agent` in the pane frame before a useful provider title appeared.

## Ownership

Yazelix owns the product label `agent` and the managed popup configuration. The popup child owns generic launch/title-preservation semantics if a true fallback primitive is required. The pane orchestrator remains the sole activity owner. Prefer an existing Zellij or popup capability; do not add a second activity signal or provider registry.

## Scope

In scope:
- Prove the title lifecycle from popup creation through launcher exec and provider OSC title updates.
- Introduce the smallest single-owner fallback that displays `agent` before or between meaningful provider titles.
- Preserve popup discovery, hide/reopen reuse, custom agent commands, and spinner-driven activity.
- Update the README LOC scorecard and CHANGELOG if project files or user-visible runtime behavior change.
- If the generic popup child must change, publish that owner change and update only the corresponding Yazelix pin.

Out of scope:
- Renaming the internal `agent_popup` identity unless the implementation proves it is user-visible and safe to separate.
- Provider-specific icons, colors, branding, title rewriting, settings, or a general pane-theme system.
- Changing tab names, activity markers, spinner recognition, popup lifecycle, or agent selection order.
- Modifying the Zellij fork when the existing popup/runtime boundary can express the contract.

## Implementation Notes

1. First test whether assigning the configured pane title during launch still permits later terminal OSC title updates.
2. If it does, use the existing popup title path and keep `preserve_terminal_title` behavior intact.
3. If it does not, add the smallest generic fallback-title semantic in the popup owner rather than inserting delays, polling, shell wrappers, or provider-specific title lists.
4. Keep the visible fallback `agent`; semantic provider titles may replace it naturally.
5. Add one focused lifecycle regression covering fallback, spinner replacement, and return to a non-busy title where the owning layer can test it deterministically.

## Verification

- Focused tests for the selected owner should exit 0 and prove fallback-title behavior without suppressing terminal-title updates.
- `cargo test --manifest-path /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij-popup/Cargo.toml --lib` should exit 0 if the popup child changes.
- `cargo test --manifest-path /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator/Cargo.toml --lib` should exit 0 if the activity boundary changes; avoid changing it unless required.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0.
- `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix --profile <tmp>` should install successfully.
- Manual dogfooding should show `agent` instead of the store path on initial open, preserve a provider spinner title while busy, clear the tab activity marker when idle, and reuse the hidden pane normally.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the proposed fallback prevents later provider OSC titles from appearing.
- Stop if activity would need polling, focus acknowledgement, timing delays, or a second owner.
- Stop before changing the Zellij fork or building a general title framework; record the missing primitive and request a narrower ownership decision.
- Stop if the fix breaks popup identity matching, hidden-pane reuse, custom commands, or native tab naming.

## Maintenance Notes

Effort: S if existing title semantics suffice; M if the popup child needs one generic fallback primitive. Risk: MED because terminal titles currently carry activity. Confidence: HIGH in the visual defect and user contract, MED in the exact implementation owner until the lifecycle experiment is run.

Rejected alternatives: displaying the provider executable path, permanently pinning `agent` and losing spinner titles, rewriting every provider title, sleeping before emitting OSC escapes, and adding a user-facing title setting for one fixed product role.
notes: Implemented in Yazelix main: yzx-agent emits one OSC 0 fallback title (agent) before exec. Custom agent.command and agent.args are positional launcher argv, so automatic and custom panes share the fallback and stable orchestrator marker. Provider OSC titles remain later in the byte stream and replace it naturally. Popup child and Zellij fork are unchanged; the obsolete custom marker rewrite was deleted. Fresh-eyes simplification removed the unexposed delimiter protocol, unreachable auto-provider argument forwarding, and duplicate custom/provider exec paths (21 Rust LOC). Focused contracts cover fallback -> spinner -> idle ordering, custom argv preservation, and every built-in provider argv. Automated flake/build/profile checks passed; manual initial-title, spinner/activity-clear, and hide/reopen dogfood remains.
close_reason: Completed; manual runtime dogfooding passed

title: Publish Nova aarch64-darwin outputs to Cachix
description:
## Why

GitHub issue #666 identified that Nova's main-branch cache workflow publishes only x86_64-linux. The existing Darwin smoke and version-gate jobs build the canonical aarch64-darwin full package, runtime package, and Home Manager check, but do not configure Cachix, so those store paths remain unavailable from yazelix.cachix.org.

## Contract

On canonical main-branch pushes and manual dispatch, a guarded macos-15 job publishes packages.aarch64-darwin.yazelix, packages.aarch64-darwin.runtime, and checks.aarch64-darwin.home_manager through the existing Yazelix Cachix cache. Missing credentials skip publishing clearly. Successful runs verify that each top-level output has a reachable narinfo. Existing Darwin smoke and version-gate ownership remains unchanged.

## Scope

- Extend the existing Publish Nix Cache workflow rather than creating another workflow.
- Follow Nova's current token guard, canonical-repository guard, Nix installer, resource limits, and Cachix action versions.
- Configure the Cachix client/auth through the action but disable its asynchronous post-job push; synchronously `cachix push` the three returned paths before probing them.
- Build without modifying flake.lock and retain useful build logs.
- Probe the narinfo for every returned top-level aarch64-darwin output with bounded retries for cache propagation.
- Keep desktop-entry and runtime behavior checks in the existing Darwin workflows.
- Update current-state documentation/scorecard required by repository policy.

## Guardrails

- Do not copy obsolete Classic package names.
- Do not alter package outputs, Home Manager behavior, release commands, or Cachix trust configuration.
- Do not weaken Linux cache publishing or merge unrelated Darwin validation changes.
- Do not require the secret for forks; report a clear skipped publish.

## Verification

Validate workflow syntax/structure locally, exercise repository contract checks, evaluate flake outputs, and run the cheapest exact Nix check that covers workflow files and advertised outputs. Live cache publication and HTTP 200 evidence complete on the first canonical workflow run.
acceptance_criteria: The Publish Nix Cache workflow has a canonical-repository-only macos-15 job guarded by CACHIX_AUTH_TOKEN. It installs Nix with bounded resources, configures cachix/cachix-action@v17 for authenticated cache access without an asynchronous post-job push, and builds packages.aarch64-darwin.yazelix, packages.aarch64-darwin.runtime, and checks.aarch64-darwin.home_manager with --no-write-lock-file and build logs. Exactly three returned top-level store paths are pushed synchronously and then checked at yazelix.cachix.org by its narinfo hash with bounded retries. Existing Linux publishing, Darwin smoke, version-gate, and desktop-entry checks remain intact. Focused workflow/contracts checks and flake evaluation pass; a canonical main/manual run provides final publication evidence.
notes: Fresh-eyes review: keep the existing Linux Cachix action unchanged. The Darwin action alone sets skipPush so its explicit cachix push completes before narinfo probes; a misplaced skipPush on Linux was corrected before handoff. Rejected a matrix job, YAML anchors, and a local shared action: they save repeated setup text but obscure the different Linux daemon-push and Darwin synchronous-push lifecycles, change job identity, or add another owner without locally runnable end-to-end proof. The final shell keeps the exact three-path guard and HTTP 200 checks; only redundant formatting and an upstream-impossible empty-line branch were removed.
close_reason: Completed and verified working

title: Improve Ratconfig light-mode contrast
description:
## Why

Several Ratconfig elements are difficult to read in light appearance because foreground, border, status, and selection colors do not consistently contrast with the light background. The config UI should remain legible without users having to switch Mars back to dark mode.

## Evidence

- Runtime dogfooding report on 2026-07-14: some config-popup elements often have poor contrast in light mode.
- `../ratconfig/src/render.rs:34-59` defines one `ConfigUiThemePalette`, but the light palette still uses named terminal colors such as `Color::Gray` for borders.
- `../ratconfig/src/render.rs:1088-1121` remaps only a fixed set of named colors; every unmatched color passes through unchanged, while many renderer call sites still use hard-coded named colors.
- `../ratconfig/src/render.rs:1656-1667` only proves that plain text and the selected background are not white. It does not defend readable semantic roles or selected-row combinations.

## Scope

### In scope

- Reproduce and list the concrete low-contrast elements in Ratconfig under Mars light appearance, including normal rows, selected rows, borders, metadata, status/error text, and detail/help text.
- Keep Ratconfig's centralized light/dark palette as the single owner and route the failing elements through semantic palette roles.
- Choose light-mode colors that remain distinguishable on both the panel background and selection background.
- Preserve dark-mode behavior unless a shared semantic-role correction is required.
- Publish the Ratconfig child change, update only the locked Ratconfig revision in Yazelix, and update the Yazelix README LOC scorecard and CHANGELOG if project/runtime files change.

### Out of scope

- A user-configurable theme system, Mars palette changes, terminal-wide color remapping, product-specific branches in Ratconfig, or a duplicate palette in Yazelix.
- Claiming universal WCAG compliance from raw RGB values alone; terminal rendering and transparency still require dogfooding.
- Adding a color/contrast dependency for a small fixed palette.

## Implementation Notes

1. Capture the failing semantic roles before changing colors.
2. Extend or simplify `ConfigUiThemePalette` and `themed_style` so renderer call sites express roles rather than scattered light-mode exceptions.
3. Prefer deleting hard-coded role colors over adding per-widget overrides.
4. Add one focused renderer contract that exercises representative normal, selected, status, and detail roles in light appearance. Assert meaningful separation/readability, not a mirror of every literal RGB value.
5. Keep the generic Ratconfig boundary: no Yazelix tab names, paths, or product policy in the child.

## Verification

- In Ratconfig: `cargo fmt --all -- --check` and `cargo test` pass.
- Focused renderer coverage fails on the current low-contrast role combinations and passes after the palette correction.
- Manual Mars dogfooding in light appearance confirms readable normal rows, selected rows, borders, metadata, errors/status, and details at the config popup's normal transparency.
- After publishing and locking Ratconfig in Yazelix: `nix flake check`, `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs`, and a temporary-profile install pass.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop and capture a screenshot plus terminal color data if the problem is caused by Mars compositing rather than Ratconfig's emitted styles; move that failing layer to the correct owner.
- Stop if the fix requires a second theme/config surface or product-specific logic in Ratconfig.
- Stop if improved light contrast materially regresses dark appearance; separate the semantic roles before proceeding.

## Maintenance Notes

Owner: Ratconfig's renderer palette; Yazelix only consumes a published revision.

Effort: S/M. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.

Rejected alternatives: scattered per-widget light-mode overrides, changing Mars globally, duplicating the palette in Yazelix, and preserving exact RGB literals with mirror tests.
close_reason: Duplicate moved to ratconfig-light-mode-contrast-nh8. Ratconfig owns the renderer palette and semantic color roles; Yazelix remains the downstream dependency-pin and product-integration owner.

title: Dismiss visible popups when toggling the sidebar
description:
## Why

The sidebar toggle currently competes with a visible full-screen popup. The useful intent is contextual: dismiss the popup and reveal the sidebar if needed, without accidentally closing an already-open sidebar.

## User-visible contract

For the packaged sidebar-toggle key:

- No visible Yazelix popup: preserve the current sidebar toggle behavior.
- Visible Yazelix popup plus open sidebar: dismiss the popup and leave the sidebar open.
- Visible Yazelix popup plus closed sidebar: dismiss the popup, open the sidebar, and restore the existing sidebar focus behavior.
- Unrelated user floating panes are not dismissed.

## Evidence

- `defaults/zellij/config.kdl:124` sends `toggle_sidebar` directly to the pane orchestrator.
- `../yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator/src/layout.rs:56-119` already owns sidebar open/closed resolution and post-layout focus.
- Its pane cache records title, floating, and suppressed state in `src/panes.rs:40-52,305-327`.
- `src/horizontal_focus_contract.rs:34-35,258-263` already recognizes visible packaged popup panes as floating, unsuppressed terminal panes whose titles end in `_popup`.
- The generic popup child owns popup lifecycle policies and hooks; `../yazelix-zellij-popup/src/main.rs:344-363` distinguishes close-policy and hide-policy popups when displacing them.

## Scope

### In scope

- Extend the pane-orchestrator sidebar command with one pure decision contract covering popup visibility and sidebar visibility.
- Dismiss only the visible packaged Yazelix popup identified from the active tab's pane manifest.
- Preserve the current no-popup sidebar behavior and existing open/close layout/focus plans.
- Preserve keep-alive state for hide-policy popups, including the config popup once its stateful-toggle bead lands.
- Ensure any popup lifecycle hook required by the packaged integration still runs through one owner.
- Publish any changed child first, update only its main-repository lock, and update the README LOC scorecard and CHANGELOG.

### Out of scope

- Hiding every floating pane, changing popup geometry, redesigning sidebar layouts, or teaching the popup plugin sidebar geometry.
- A new user setting or keybinding.
- Coupling the generic popup child to Yazelix's layout names or duplicating popup close/hide hooks in two plugins.
- Folding in optional hide-on-file-open or columns-layout work.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add a small resolver whose inputs are `visible_packaged_popup` and `sidebar_is_closed`, and whose outputs describe popup dismissal plus the existing sidebar action.
2. Reuse the orchestrator's active-tab pane cache and exact packaged popup identity rule. Do not infer from focus alone.
3. Prefer a semantic dismissal route that honors the popup spec's close-versus-hide behavior and hooks. If direct pane suppression bypasses required `on_hide` behavior, add the smallest generic popup dismissal message and route to it; do not copy hook execution into the orchestrator.
4. Keep ordering deterministic: the popup must be gone before sidebar focus is finalized.
5. Add no new dependency.

## Verification

- Focused child tests cover all four boundaries: no popup/open sidebar, no popup/closed sidebar, popup/open sidebar, and popup/closed sidebar.
- A non-popup floating pane remains visible.
- Config/agent hide-policy popups retain their process state; close-policy git/menu popups retain their existing lifecycle policy.
- Existing sidebar focus, collapsed-base, missing-sidebar, and no-sidebar-mode tests continue to pass.
- In each changed child: `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, `cargo test --lib`, the WASI build, and its Nix build pass.
- After the main lock/config update: `nix flake check`, `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs`, and a temporary-profile install pass.
- Manual dogfooding exercises config, agent, git, and menu popups with the sidebar initially open and closed.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if plugin-to-plugin dismissal cannot be ordered reliably in the current Zellij API; record the event trace before adding retries or sleeps.
- Stop if exact packaged popup identity cannot be distinguished from user floating panes using the existing manifest/config contract.
- Stop if the only implementation duplicates popup lifecycle policy or hooks in the pane orchestrator; move dismissal back behind one popup-owned semantic action.
- Stop before changing optional file-open/sidebar behavior or layout-family policy.

## Maintenance Notes

Owner: pane orchestrator for the contextual sidebar decision; popup plugin remains the single owner of popup lifecycle semantics.

Effort: M. Risk: MEDIUM because it coordinates two plugins and focus timing. Confidence: MEDIUM/HIGH.

Rejected alternatives: always opening the sidebar, always toggling it after hiding a popup, hiding all floating panes, hard-coding each popup in the keybinding, and duplicating popup hooks in the orchestrator.

title: Make Ratconfig field columns width-aware
description:
## Why

Ratconfig's default three-column field list reserves 18 cells for `takes effect`, 44 for `setting`, and only 28 for `value`. In the normal half-screen config popup this leaves a large empty gap after short setting labels while structured values and long paths are truncated much earlier than necessary. The list should remain a compact comparison surface, but it should use the width it actually has.

## Evidence

- Runtime dogfooding report on 2026-07-14: the Yazi tab shows a conspicuous gap before `value`, while flavor, opener, plugin, and native-file values truncate despite unused horizontal space.
- `../ratconfig/src/render.rs:11-13` hard-codes the default field widths to 18, 44, and 28.
- `../ratconfig/src/render.rs:316-338` gives the list half the terminal width and passes that rectangle into `render_list`.
- `../ratconfig/src/render.rs:338-375` knows the list's exact inner width, but headers and rows are built without using it.
- `../ratconfig/src/render.rs:613-634` pads every setting to 44 cells and truncates every value at 28.
- `crates/yzx-config/src/model.rs:147-159` supplies a custom table only for Keys, so the Yazi tab correctly exercises Ratconfig's default field layout.

## User-Visible Contract

The default `takes effect | setting | value` list stays column-aligned across a tab, avoids visible padding far beyond the tab's actual labels, and gives `value` all useful remaining width. Values may still end in an ellipsis because the list is a preview, but unused setting padding must not force premature truncation. Layout remains readable without overflow at representative wide, medium, and narrow popup widths.

## Scope

In scope:
- Change Ratconfig's default field-list geometry in `src/render.rs` to resolve widths from the available list width and tab content.
- Keep column positions stable while moving selection and while entering or clearing search; avoid distracting width jitter.
- Give the value column the remaining usable width after bounded status and setting columns.
- Preserve explicit host-supplied `ConfigUiListTable` behavior and Advanced status-table behavior.
- Add focused renderer contracts for width allocation, alignment, truncation, and narrow boundaries.
- Publish the Ratconfig child change, update only the locked Ratconfig revision in Yazelix, and update Yazelix's CHANGELOG and LOC scorecard as required.

Out of scope:
- Do not remove or abbreviate the `takes effect` column.
- Do not add horizontal scrolling, resizable panes, a detail-only mode, or a six-column TOML table to Yazi.
- Do not change the 50/50 body split in this slice.
- Do not change colors or include the separate light-mode contrast bead.
- Do not add Yazelix/Yazi-specific layout rules to Ratconfig.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add one small pure width resolver whose inputs are available inner width and the default field rows for the selected tab.
2. Use explicit minimums and caps for status and setting; allocate the remainder to value. Derive stable tab widths rather than selection-dependent widths.
