title: Stabilize Nova beta, release v1, and privatize the former development repository
description:
## Why

The canonical swap intentionally begins as a beta because repository, packaging, renamed interfaces, canonical paths, and real terminal integration bugs surface most reliably after canonical installs and upgrades. Nova graduates by evidence rather than elapsed time, and the development repository remains public until canonical main is independently complete.

## Evidence

- The chosen release line is Yazelix Nova v1 with a roughly one-to-two-week beta observation window.
- The final Classic bridge tag remains the rollback tag.
- The cutover bead owns `nova-v1.0.0-beta.1`, `yzx`, and canonical `yazelix` roots.
- The main repository owns the v17-to-Nova canonical-root and rollback preparation.
- Existing macOS validation owns trusted Mars-path acceptance.
- former-development user config adoption is explicitly unsupported and is not a release gate.

## User-Visible Contract

During beta, canonical main identifies itself as Yazelix Nova 1.0.0-beta.N, collects regressions, and documents the final Classic bridge tag rollback using the main-prepared contract. Beta fixes land on canonical main and may cut sequential namespaced beta tags. Stable `nova-v1.0.0` is released only after exit gates pass. the former development repository becomes private only after canonical main contains its history and no installation, flake input, workflow, documentation, release artifact, or source link depends on the public Nova repository.

## Scope

In scope:
- Run a roughly one-to-two-week beta, extending it when exit criteria are not met.
- Triage and fix P0/P1 regressions from fresh installs, main-prepared v17 upgrades/rollback, Home Manager, Linux, and the declared macOS floor.
- Cut `nova-v1.0.0-beta.N` tags as needed with exact release metadata and full-range release notes.
- Keep visible the final Classic bridge tag rollback instructions throughout beta.
- Audit docs, workflows, flake inputs/locks, badges, issue templates, package metadata, source links, and release artifacts for the former development repository dependencies.
- Run the complete release/Version Gate, cut `nova-v1.0.0`, and publish stable Nova v1.
- Verify both histories from stable main, then make the former development repository private.
- Update open planning beads whose live command/path wording must become `yzx`/Nova; do not rewrite closed history mechanically.

Out of scope:
- Do not support importing or adopting former-development config.
- Do not duplicate v17 upgrade/rollback logic owned by main.
- Do not make every P2/P3 roadmap item a stable blocker.
- Do not delete either repository or rewrite history.
- Do not delete v17 tags/releases, create `legacy` branding, advance the emergency-only `classic` branch for ordinary features, or introduce a former-development-command alias.
- Do not broaden terminal/platform support solely to avoid labeling an honest limitation.
- Do not begin AppImage/Homebrew/Nix-free distribution before their prerequisites.

## Implementation Notes

1. Track beta P0/P1 issues with platform, reproduction, owner, fix version, and verification.
2. Time is a minimum observation window, not acceptance. Extend after material runtime/path fixes.
3. Stable gates: no open P0/P1 Nova regressions; fresh install passes; the main-owned v17 upgrade and tag rollback contract passes; `yzx` core commands pass; Yazi/Helix/Git/config/popups pass dogfood; Home Manager, Linux, and declared macOS checks pass; docs and rollback remain current.
4. Before changing repository visibility, verify canonical main contains the prepared Nova commit and builds without authenticated Nova access.
5. Keep tags namespaced `nova-v1...` while product output displays semantic version.

## Verification

- Each beta fix runs focused checks plus relevant canonical flake/package gates.
- `nix flake check`, `nix flake show --all-systems`, `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs`, and a temporary profile install pass for release candidates.
- Version Gate passes for stable Nova v1.
- Manual dogfood covers `yzx launch`, enter, config, doctor, status, tutor, Yazi-to-Helix opens, LazyGit editor return, and popup lifecycle.
- Main-prepared v17 upgrade and the final Classic bridge tag rollback checks pass against canonical `yazelix` roots.
- Both the final Classic bridge tag and the prepared Nova commit are ancestors of `nova-v1.0.0`.
- A clean unauthenticated canonical clone at `nova-v1.0.0` evaluates/builds after Nova becomes private.
- GitHub reports Nova private only after preceding checks pass.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop stable release while any confirmed P0/P1 Nova regression is open.
- Stop if the main-owned v17 upgrade/rollback contract is absent or failing.
- Stop if a declared supported-platform gate is missing.
- Stop before privatizing Nova if any public/canonical surface still requires it or its history is unreachable.
- Stop if beta is ending only because one or two weeks elapsed.

## Maintenance Notes

Effort spans the beta observation period plus fixes; risk MED/HIGH, confidence HIGH. Rejected: date-only graduation, Nova config adoption, permanent `yazelix-nova` roots, duplicating main compatibility work, hiding Nova immediately, deleting Nova, unnamespaced v1 tags, and optional roadmap work blocking stable indefinitely.
notes: Published Yazelix Nova 1.0.0-beta.2 on 2026-07-17 at tag commit 3253e14b4a39f19be3031dfa8cd64c4ff4d3184b. Local nix flake check, all-system flake show, full package build, temporary profile install/status, and installed-profile refresh passed. Canonical CI run 29620352283, Linux/aarch64-darwin cache publication run 29620352272, and Version Gate run 29620360304 all succeeded at the exact tag commit. The task remains open: real macOS interactive Mars/Home Manager evidence in yazelix-8u5-5-fl0 still blocks stable v1.

title: Confirm canonical Nova Home Manager and Mars on macOS
description:
## Why

The pre-cutover macOS gate can prove Nova's published staging package, Home Manager module, Mars, and managed workflow on real Darwin hardware. It cannot prove the atomically renamed canonical `yzx` package and `github:luccahuguet/yazelix` install path before those surfaces exist. Stable Nova v1 still needs that final canonical confirmation, and GitHub issue 577 must remain open until it happens.

## User outcome

During the Nova beta, a real macOS Home Manager or nix-darwin user installs canonical Yazelix, activates the canonical module, runs the `yzx` command floor, launches Mars, and uses managed Zellij, Yazi, and Helix without Linux-only helpers or desktop entries.

## Scope

- Start only after the canonical cutover publishes a Nova beta from `luccahuguet/yazelix`.
- Reuse the smallest test plan and environment evidence from the staging macOS validation bead.
- Test a published canonical input without local overrides.
- Verify `yzx help`, `yzx status --json`, `yzx doctor`, `yzx enter`, and `yzx launch` from Terminal.
- Verify Home Manager-owned canonical config is declarative/read-only and not mutated.
- Verify Mars keyboard input plus managed Zellij, Yazi, Helix, and Yazi-to-editor open.
- Confirm Darwin receives no Linux desktop entry or Linux-only runtime dependency.
- Record exact version, architecture, install shape, commands, and results in GitHub issue 577 through the canonical main issue-mapping bead.
- Close GitHub issue 577 only after the macOS user confirms the canonical beta.

## Out of scope

- Do not block the beta source swap on a public surface that does not exist until that swap.
- Do not add compatibility for the former development command or repository paths.
- Do not broaden macOS or terminal support beyond the tested contract.
- Do not close from cross-evaluation alone.

## Verification

- Canonical Darwin package and Home Manager boundaries evaluate/build without overrides.
- A real macOS activation exposes only the canonical `yzx`.
- The command floor and Mars-managed workflow pass on real hardware.
- Config ownership remains declarative and Linux-only surfaces are absent.
- GitHub issue 577 contains the evidence and is closed.
- Any failure has an owner-correct P0/P1 beta follow-up.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the canonical beta is unpublished or the test uses a local override.
- Stop if a non-macOS agent attempts to substitute evaluation for manual runtime evidence.
- Stop stable Nova v1 while this canonical confirmation or any resulting P0/P1 fix remains incomplete.

## Maintenance Notes

This is an internal post-cutover release gate. The main repository's `yazelix-8u5-5-fl0` bead remains the sole owner of GitHub issue 577 and its `external_ref`; do not duplicate that mapping here.

title: Launch Yazelix Nova v1
description:
## Why

Yazelix Nova is established in the development repository while temporary command and repository namespaces let it coexist with public Yazelix Classic. Canonical `luccahuguet/yazelix` carries the established project identity, community, issues, stars, releases, and install URL. Nova must become the canonical product without inheriting the roughly 3,800-commit Classic graph on default `main`.

## Outcome

- Product: Yazelix Nova.
- Public command after cutover: `yzx`, with no former-development-command compatibility alias.
- Config root: `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/yazelix`; state root: `$XDG_DATA_HOME/yazelix`.
- Release line: `1.0.0-beta.N`, then `1.0.0`, with namespaced `nova-v1.0.0-beta.N` and `nova-v1.0.0` tags.
- Canonical repository: `luccahuguet/yazelix`, retaining its GitHub identity and replacing default `main` with the clean Nova lineage through an exact-SHA guarded force-with-lease.
- `classic` is a frozen archival branch at `v17.9`, the last ordinary Classic release before broad Nova convergence.
- `v17.12` remains the immutable final Classic migration and rollback bridge. No `classic-bridge` branch exists.
- Existing Classic tags and releases remain unchanged. Any final post-v17.12 Classic evidence commit must remain reachable through a non-product archival ref recorded by the cutover; it does not create another maintained branch.
- Former-development config and state are ignored and unsupported.
- the former development repository becomes private only after canonical main is independently complete and stable.

## Program Slices

1. Brand development Nova while retaining `yzx` — completed.
2. Converge Classic config and publish the v17.12 bridge — completed.
3. Finish beta onboarding and the remaining cutover preflight.
4. Prepare one complete canonical Nova commit on the Nova lineage, replace canonical `main` under a guarded maintenance window, and publish the beta.
5. Stabilize the beta, release Nova v1, and make the former development repository private.

## Scope

In scope:
- Keep the canonical Yazelix repository, issues, stars, forks, URLs, releases, and tags.
- Preserve Classic through explicit refs rather than making Classic commits ancestors of Nova main.
- Consume the main-owned v17.12 migration contract without adding a Classic parser or compatibility owner to Nova.
- Atomically rename former development commands and paths, packages, apps, helpers, environment contracts, docs, CI, and release identity to canonical Nova surfaces.
- Rehearse the guarded main replacement and restore branch protection and automation in the same maintenance window.

Out of scope:
- No former-development user migration, former-development-command alias, equal Classic product line, `classic-bridge` branch, two-parent history merge, orphan rewrite, repository rename dance, or archived canonical repository.
- Do not port Nova into Classic piecemeal or make unrelated P2/P3 work block beta.
- Do not implement AppImage, Homebrew, or the Nix-free installer in this program.

## Verification

- Canonical main contains the recorded Nova root and prepared Nova commit, but `v17.12` is not an ancestor of main.
- Remote `classic` peels exactly to `v17.9`; `v17.12` remains fetchable; no remote `classic-bridge` exists.
- The final pre-cutover Classic head remains reachable through a recorded Classic ref if it is newer than `v17.12`.
- The canonical default-branch commit count matches the Nova lineage plus reviewed cutover commits instead of the union of Classic and Nova histories.
- Canonical packages expose only `yzx` as the public command, and use canonical config/state roots.
- Existing GitHub identity and public issue/release state remain attached to `luccahuguet/yazelix`.
- The public release documents v17.12 as the migration/rollback bridge and v17.9 as the frozen ordinary Classic branch.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if either source repository is dirty, unpushed, or changes during rehearsal.
- Stop if the expected old canonical main SHA cannot be protected by force-with-lease.
- Stop if any Classic tag, required archival ref, open public issue mapping, release, or rollback artifact would be lost.
- Stop if branch protection and required Nova checks cannot be restored immediately.
- Stop if canonical main retains a live dependency on the former development repository or Nova reads former-development user state.

## Maintenance Notes

Priority P1 because this program controls public identity and the one-time history boundary. Rejected: abandoning the 1,113-star canonical repository for the former development repository, reusing a renamed repository path, two-parent history union, raw `--force`, former-development user migration, permanent Nova-specific roots, and a standing v17.12 bridge branch.
notes:
Canonical config decision: Nova inherits packaged defaults for absent root fields and persists only explicit user intent. Final Classic migrates legacy values explicitly; Nova consumes the resulting canonical file without another migration owner.

Repository decision 2026-07-13: keep `luccahuguet/yazelix`, but replace default `main` with the clean Nova lineage using exact-SHA `--force-with-lease`. Preserve ordinary Classic at frozen branch `classic` -> `v17.9`; preserve the supported migration/rollback bridge as immutable tag `v17.12`; do not create `classic-bridge`. Preserve any post-tag Classic evidence through a non-product archival ref if required. The canonical repository identity, issues, stars, forks, releases, and URLs remain in place.

title: Add AppImage Linux distribution
description:
## Why

The user wants an AppImage packaging track as a Linux non-Nix option. AppImage can provide a single-file Linux desktop artifact that users can download and run without a distro package manager. For Yazelix, this should be treated as a wrapper around the relocatable runtime bundle, not as a new source-of-truth package system.

## User-Visible Behavior

A Linux user can download a Yazelix AppImage, mark it executable, and run it:

```sh
chmod +x Yazelix-x86_64.AppImage
./Yazelix-x86_64.AppImage
```

The AppImage launches the Mars-backed Yazelix app path when possible and exposes a way to run the `yzx` CLI from the same artifact or installed desktop integration. It should not require Nix on the user's machine.

## Source Context

- Existing prerequisite bead: `yazelix-nix-free-installer-nx4-a48` designs the Nix-built relocatable release bundle and Nix-free install path.
- Official AppImage docs describe the format as a Linux application packaged into one file that can be downloaded, made executable, and run without installation.
- AppImage packaging generally uses an AppDir layout plus `appimagetool`; Yazelix should reuse the relocatable runtime layout rather than inventing a separate filesystem contract.

## Scope

In scope:
- Build a Linux AppImage from the relocatable Yazelix runtime bundle.
- Start with the most useful Linux architecture, likely `x86_64-linux`, then add `aarch64-linux` only if the runtime and toolchain support it cleanly.
- Include Mars app launch, the final public `yzx` CLI, desktop entry metadata, icon, packaged runtime tools/assets, Zellij plugins/WASMs, and config/layout assets.
- Define how CLI invocation works from an AppImage artifact, such as `--appimage-extract-and-run`, a wrapper symlink installed by optional integration, or a documented `Yazelix.AppImage -- <yzx args>` style if feasible.
- Verify that `yzx help`, `yzx enter`, and `yzx launch` or their AppImage equivalents run without Nix store paths.
- Document install/run/update/uninstall expectations honestly: download-and-run first, desktop integration later if needed.
- Keep AppImage as a Linux package option, not the canonical release system.

Out of scope:
- Do not make AppImage the source of truth for runtime composition.
- Do not add Flatpak or Snap in this bead.
- Do not solve macOS packaging here.
- Do not package Ghostty or broaden terminal support for AppImage.
- Do not add a full auto-updater in the first slice.
- Do not assume sandbox isolation; AppImage should run as the user and use normal user config paths.

## Implementation Notes

1. Wait until the relocatable runtime bundle contract exists and proves the runtime does not depend on embedded `/nix/store` paths.
2. Use Nix as the build factory for the AppDir/AppImage if that keeps dependencies deterministic.
3. Map the relocatable Yazelix bundle into an AppDir with a clear `AppRun`, `.desktop`, icon, and internal runtime root.
4. Decide whether AppRun defaults to `yzx launch` and how to pass through CLI args to `yzx` without surprising users.
5. Test both direct execution and extracted execution, because AppImage/FUSE behavior can differ across distributions.
6. Treat desktop integration as optional unless the first AppImage usability test proves it is necessary.

## Verification

- The AppImage build command should produce a Linux AppImage artifact without manual steps.
- Running the AppImage on a Linux test machine should start the Mars-backed app path or print a clear diagnostic if GUI prerequisites are missing.
- Running the CLI passthrough path should make `yzx help` exit 0 without Nix installed.
- `yzx doctor` or `yzx status`, when implemented, should pass from the AppImage runtime.
- The runtime should not reference `/nix/store` paths after extraction except in build metadata that is not used at runtime.
- AppImage desktop metadata should show the expected name, icon, and categories.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the runtime is not relocatable without embedded Nix store paths.
- Stop if AppImage requires broad invasive changes to Mars, Zellij, or the runtime launcher before the core release path is stable.
- Stop if FUSE/AppImage runtime behavior makes CLI use unreliable and no simple extracted-run fallback works.
- Stop if building the AppImage starts duplicating flake package composition or release artifact layout.
- Stop if this starts becoming Flatpak/Snap/sandbox packaging work.

## Maintenance Notes

Rejected alternative: Flatpak first. Yazelix wants host filesystem, host shells, agent/editor workflows, and terminal integration; Flatpak sandbox policy would become the main task. Rejected alternative: Snap first. Strict confinement and classic-confinement review do not fit the first Linux package path as well as AppImage.

title: Add Homebrew tap and bottle distribution
description:
## Why

A Homebrew install is the cleanest non-Nix package-manager experience for developer users, especially on macOS. The user wants a Homebrew bottle path in addition to the existing Nix-free installer/release-bundle plan. This should wrap the same relocatable Yazelix runtime rather than creating a second runtime packaging implementation.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user can install the public Yazelix package through a tap:

```sh
brew install luccahuguet/yazelix/yzx
```

The installed `yzx` command runs without requiring Nix on the user's machine. `yzx help`, `yzx enter`, and `yzx launch` work from the Homebrew install. Upgrades and uninstall use normal Homebrew workflows.

## Source Context

- Existing prerequisite bead: `yazelix-nix-free-installer-nx4-a48` designs the Nix-built relocatable release bundle and Nix-free install path.
- Official Homebrew bottle docs describe bottles as binary packages produced with `brew install --build-bottle <formula>` and `brew bottle <formula>`.
- Official Homebrew tap docs describe taps as external Homebrew formula repositories and note that bottles can be built by GitHub Actions for taps.

## Scope

In scope:
- Create a Homebrew tap/formula plan for the final public `yzx` package.
- Decide whether the formula builds from source, installs the Nix-built relocatable release artifact, or uses a tiny source formula that Homebrew bottles into platform-specific artifacts.
- Prefer reusing the relocatable runtime bundle from `yazelix-nix-free-installer-nx4-a48` instead of recreating package layout in Ruby.
- Support at least macOS Apple Silicon first if that is the first validated non-Nix target.
- Add Linuxbrew only if the same bottle path works without broad platform-specific patching.
- Include checks for installed `yzx help`, `yzx enter --help` or equivalent non-GUI contract, desktop/app behavior where relevant, and clean uninstall.
- Document tap usage, upgrade behavior, and the relationship between Homebrew and Nix-built release artifacts.
- Keep the final public command named `yzx`.

Out of scope:
- Do not make Homebrew the build source of truth; Nix remains the deterministic release factory unless a later decision reverses that.
- Do not package Ghostty, Neovim, or broad terminal/editor variants just because Homebrew can install them.
- Do not add Home Manager-style runtime config management through Homebrew.
- Do not make this block private Yazelix Nova iteration or core runtime cleanup.
- Do not publish to Homebrew/core in the first slice; use a tap.

## Implementation Notes

1. Wait until the relocatable runtime bundle contract exists and proves that installed binaries/configs do not depend on `/nix/store` paths.
2. Prototype the tap locally with a formula that installs the bundle into Homebrew's prefix and exposes `bin/yzx`.
3. Decide bottle hosting: tap GitHub Releases or Homebrew-supported bottle hosting for taps.
4. Keep the formula minimal. It should not encode Yazelix runtime policy beyond installation paths and metadata.
5. Use GitHub Actions for bottle generation only after local formula install/uninstall works.
6. Preserve a clear path for both full and runtime-only variants only if the flake/release bundle already exposes those variants.

## Verification

- `nix build` or the chosen release-bundle build command should produce the relocatable bundle artifact used by the formula.
- Local tap install should place a working `yzx` on `PATH` without Nix installed or referenced at runtime.
- `yzx help` should exit 0 from the Homebrew install.
- A non-GUI startup/diagnostic command, such as `yzx doctor` or `yzx status`, should exit 0 when implemented.
- Homebrew uninstall should remove the package-owned files without touching user config under `~/.config/yazelix*`.
- Bottle CI should build and publish the expected platform bottle before announcing the tap.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the runtime is not relocatable without embedded Nix store paths.
- Stop if the formula starts duplicating Yazelix package composition from `flake.nix`.
- Stop if Homebrew's sandbox/build model requires invasive runtime changes before the core release path is stable.
- Stop if the package name or canonical `yzx` surface is unsettled.

## Maintenance Notes

Rejected alternative: publish Homebrew before the relocatable bundle is stable. That creates a second unstable install surface and makes support harder. Rejected alternative: make Homebrew build every child tool from source; that recreates Nix badly and substantially increases maintenance cost.

title: Evaluate asciiaquarium as a Yazelix screen style
description: Evaluate asciiaquarium as an optional terminal-safe style owned by yazelix-screen rather than copied into Nova runtime code. Check packaging, license, terminal behavior, resize/input/exit semantics, and whether the experience is worth its closure cost.
acceptance_criteria: Record upstream identity, license, package closure, rendering/input/resize behavior, and whether yazelix-screen can own it without copied main code. End with accept/reject. If accepted, create child-owned implementation and Nova consumption beads with a published-child release gate.

title: Evaluate Channels and Television as Yazelix references
description: Identify the exact Channels and Television projects referenced by the maintainer, inspect their current behavior and ownership model, and decide whether either offers a concrete idea worth adopting in Nova. Research only until the user approves a specific product outcome; do not add packages or runtime scope from name similarity.
acceptance_criteria: Record exact project URLs, purpose, current maintenance/licensing, the relevant idea if any, and how it compares with existing Nova owners. End with adopt-one-small-idea or reject; any implementation requires a separately approved bead. Close if the intended projects cannot be identified from the public issue and maintainer context.

title: Evaluate clin-rs for a concrete Nova use case
description: Identify the intended clin-rs project, inspect its API, maintenance state, dependencies, and licensing, and compare it with Nova’s actual command/runtime owners. Proceed only if it solves a named user problem with less ownership than the current path.
acceptance_criteria: Record the exact upstream, API/use case, maintenance/licensing, dependency and Nix impact, and comparison with current Nova code. End with a concrete adopt/reject decision. Any Rust implementation records the required crate-vs-in-house decision first.

title: Add truthful agent activity decorations to Nova tabs
description:
## Why

Nova does not currently display agent activity decorations, so the carried-over title and P1 bug framing are stale. The useful feature is to show a compact, truthful busy marker on the tab whose managed agent is working while keeping native Zellij tab names entirely user-owned. Classic already fixed its rename conflict by moving presentation out of native tab names; Nova should reuse that architectural result without assuming its current popup produces the same signal.

## Evidence

- `packaging/bar-render-request.nix:10-23` asks the bar child to package and render its own `zjstatus.wasm`.
- `flake.lock` currently resolves the bar child `zjstatus` input to upstream `dj95/zjstatus@17609e4`; that upstream source has no `tab_activity_pipe_name` or `pipe_tab_activity` implementation.
- The generated Nova layout nevertheless contains `tab_activity_pipe_name`, busy marker `·`, and alert marker `✓` from `yazelix-zellij-bar/presets/yazelix_runtime_bar.template.kdl:19-22`. Those keys are inert with the packaged upstream wasm.
- `../yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator/src/status_bar_cache.rs:34-55,108-135` already produces the all-tab activity snapshot and pushes it to every known zjstatus instance without renaming tabs.
- `../yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator/src/ai_pane_activity_contract.rs:60-77` recognizes spinner-prefixed terminal titles as activity, and `src/ai_pane_activity.rs:61-125` also accepts explicit `register_ai_pane_activity` facts.
- Nova has no `register_ai_pane_activity` producer. Live `zellij action list-panes --json` inspection showed a running `codex resume` popup exposed as the fixed title `agent_popup`.
- `../yazelix-zellij-popup/src/main.rs:298-325` opens a command pane and then calls `rename_pane_with_id` with the configured popup title.
- `../yazelix-zellij/zellij-server/src/panes/terminal_pane.rs:1007-1023` returns a non-empty custom `pane_name` instead of the terminal application title, explaining why Nova masks the spinner title used by the existing detector.
- Classic published the durable presentation pieces in pane-orchestrator `8cea8ec`, bar `7ceee67`, zjstatus fork `524a6bf`, and main integration `75029436`. The fork branch `luccahuguet/zjstatus@yazelix-tab-activity-pipe` still exists.

## User-visible contract

When the managed Nova agent exposes a trustworthy working signal, its tab shows one compact busy decoration. When no trustworthy signal is available, Nova shows no activity decoration rather than equating process lifetime, popup visibility, or focus with model work. The decoration is presentation-only: explicit tab names survive activity updates and remain raw/editable in Zellij rename mode. Existing tab focus, click, create/delete, truncation, fullscreen/sync/floating indicators, and terminal-bell styling remain native zjstatus behavior.

## Scope

In scope:
- Restore or rebase the smallest activity-capable zjstatus tabs-widget extension and make the Yazelix bar package/runtime select a compatible wasm instead of emitting unsupported KDL.
- Keep the pane orchestrator as the sole tab-local activity fact owner and `pipe_tab_activity` producer.
- Establish one truthful activity input for the Nova managed agent popup. Prefer reusing the existing spinner-title contract if popup identity can remain reliable; otherwise prove the smallest explicit signal or Zellij title-seam change.
- Evaluate three bounded signal paths from source and live evidence: preserve terminal-title visibility without losing popup identity; expose the raw terminal application title separately from the custom pane name in the Yazelix Zellij fork; or consume a stable provider event through `register_ai_pane_activity`.
- Scope the first supported signal honestly. Codex spinner-title behavior is sufficient for the first slice; other configured providers get markers only when they expose the same proven contract.
- Correct stale child documentation that still describes native tab-name mutation or claims the upstream-packaged wasm supports activity keys.
- Add a packaged compatibility check so the selected `zjstatus.wasm` and generated activity KDL cannot silently drift apart again.
- Update README LOC scorecard and CHANGELOG when project/runtime files change.

Out of scope:
- Inferring activity from the long-lived agent process, popup visibility, pane focus, or the presence of a Codex subprocess.
- Reintroducing `rename_tab_with_id`, tab-name suffix persistence, a command-widget replacement tab strip, broad pane polling, or a new status-bar implementation.
- Provider-specific compatibility claims without a real working/idle signal.
- The hidden or unfocused-tab clearing regression; that is a separate dependent bead.
- Agent command-center, notifications, popup redesign, or general terminal-title API expansion beyond the smallest demonstrated signal gap.

## Implementation Notes

1. First make a controlled Nova popup produce the same working/idle signal used in Classic and inspect the pane manifest, activity snapshot, and pipe payload.
2. Rebase or validate zjstatus fork `524a6bf` against the currently selected upstream before pinning it. Preserve live `TabInfo.name`, raw rename-mode labels, tab-id keyed activity, JSON containing `::`, click behavior, and truncation.
3. Choose the smallest signal seam that preserves popup identity. If removing the custom rename is considered, prove all popup toggle, displacement, cwd-restart, sidebar interaction, and hidden keep-alive paths still identify the pane.
4. If a Zellij API field is needed, expose the raw terminal title separately; do not overload the user/custom pane name.
5. Keep marker glyph/style configuration in `yazelix-zellij-bar`; keep activity facts and reduction in the pane orchestrator.
6. Add no new production or development crate.

## Verification

- Focused pane-orchestrator tests prove facts are keyed by stable tab id and pipe snapshots never mutate native names.
- Focused zjstatus-fork tests prove busy/idle/alert parsing, live-name precedence over stale payload names, rename-mode raw names, JSON containing `::`, tab movement, truncation, and existing bell behavior.
- Focused popup or Zellij tests prove the selected activity signal remains visible without losing packaged popup identity.
- A package-level check proves the rendered activity keys are paired with an activity-capable `zjstatus.wasm`.
- Relevant child formatting, tests, WASI builds, and Nix builds pass before child publication.
- `nix flake check`, `nix flake show --all-systems`, `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs`, and a temporary-profile install pass.
- After `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix`, a fresh multi-tab dogfood pass shows the marker only on the working agent tab and confirms tab rename during activity.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the proposed source cannot distinguish working from an idle interactive agent process.
- Stop if exposing terminal-title activity breaks popup identity, hide/reopen state, cwd retarget restart, or unrelated floating-pane behavior.
- Stop if the renderer path writes activity into `TabInfo.name` or replaces native tab semantics with polling.
- Stop if the old zjstatus fork cannot be rebased narrowly; review the minimal extension against current upstream instead of pinning stale code blindly.
- Stop before claiming support for providers that do not expose a tested activity signal.

## Maintenance Notes

This issue is deliberately reclassified from a P1 bug to a P2 feature. Classic is a reference implementation, not a port target. The durable ownership rule survives: native tab names are user data; activity is a fact; the bar renders the fact.

Effort: M across integration seams. Risk: MEDIUM, concentrated in obtaining a truthful popup signal. Confidence: HIGH about the missing renderer pin and masked-title cause; MEDIUM on the cheapest signal seam until live instrumentation chooses it.

Rejected alternatives: process-lifetime markers, focus-driven markers, tab renaming, broad `list-panes` polling, and a second tab strip.
acceptance_criteria:
- A managed agent with a proven working signal decorates only its own tab with the configured busy marker; idle or unknown agents do not show a false marker.
- Explicit native tab names remain unchanged in Zellij state while activity starts, updates, and ends, and rename mode displays and edits the raw live tab name.
- The packaged bar uses a zjstatus implementation that consumes `pipe_tab_activity`; generated activity KDL is not paired with an upstream wasm that ignores it.
- The selected Nova popup activity source is documented and tested, preserves popup identity/lifecycle, and does not infer work from process lifetime, visibility, or focus.
- Live tab identity, focus, click switching, creation/deletion, movement, truncation, fullscreen/sync/floating indicators, and bell styling remain intact.
- Focused tests cover tab-id keyed snapshots, stale payload names versus live names, JSON protocol framing, rename mode, and the chosen signal seam.
- Child changes are published before lock updates, exact child and main checks pass, the installed runtime is refreshed, and a fresh multi-tab dogfood pass confirms working decoration plus rename-during-activity.
notes: Implemented the activity slice without native tab-name mutation or a Zellij fork. Published popup a13ef50 and 72b898a (preserve and reveal terminal application titles), pane orchestrator 5cd75d4 and 8d4712a (managed-command scoping plus reliable all-plugin activity broadcast), bar facd2e4 (accurate consumer contract), and reused the focused zjstatus extension 524a6bf. Main pins those children, wires the managed agent command marker, preserves the agent terminal title, and checks the packaged wasm for activity support. Focused child tests passed (popup 32, orchestrator 79, zjstatus activity/name/protocol tests), along with child WASI and Nix builds. Main nix flake check, nix flake show --all-systems, nix build .#yazelix, temporary-profile install, and installed-profile refresh passed. Fresh installed two-tab dogfood showed only the working agent tab decorated, preserved dogfood-renamed in native state when renamed during activity, cleared the marker on idle while unfocused, and preserved popup pane identity through hide/reopen. No process-lifetime, focus, visibility, or tab-renaming inference was added.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add semantic config for managed shell integrations
description:
## Why

Yazelix Nova currently initializes Carapace, Zoxide, and Starship inside the managed Nushell runtime by default. That is the right default, but users may eventually need a small supported way to disable or inspect those managed shell integrations without replacing the whole Nushell config or editing packaged files.

## Current Contract

Inside `yzx`-managed Nushell:

- Carapace completions are generated and sourced by default.
- Zoxide init is generated and sourced by default.
- Starship owns the left and right prompt by default.
- Optional user files under `~/.config/yazelix/nu/env.nu` and `config.nu` are layered after packaged config.
- Normal user Nushell config is not loaded by default.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user can use the Yazelix semantic config surface, and later Ratconfig, to inspect and optionally toggle managed shell integrations such as Carapace, Zoxide, and Starship. Defaults preserve the current curated shell behavior.

Possible first settings:

```toml
[shell]
carapace = true
zoxide = true
starship = true
```

Names are illustrative; choose final names only when implementing the contract.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add the smallest semantic config surface for managed Nushell integrations if/when a real need appears.
- Keep defaults equal to current behavior: Carapace, Zoxide, and Starship enabled.
- Route all validation through the shared Yazelix config core.
- Make `runtime/yzx-nu.rs` or its owner consume the settings without loading ambient user shell config.
- Surface the settings in Ratconfig only if the config core owns them.
- Add focused checks for defaults, toggling each integration, and generated Nushell config contents.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard if implemented.

Out of scope:
- Do not add Bash, Zsh, Fish, or arbitrary managed-shell selection in this bead.
- Do not import the user's normal Nushell config.
- Do not build a generic shell plugin manager.
- Do not translate native shell config through Ratconfig.
- Do not add this setting before someone actually needs to disable or inspect an integration.
- Do not weaken the current managed shell reproducibility contract.

## Implementation Notes

1. Prefer booleans over a generic plugin list.
2. Keep generated Nushell config deterministic and easy to test.
3. Preserve `PATH` setup so enabled tools remain available inside managed Nushell.
4. If disabling Starship, define the resulting prompt behavior explicitly before coding.
5. Carapace support remains a curated managed-shell default, not a promise to support every external shell.

## Verification

- Config contract check proves missing settings default to current enabled behavior.
- Check proves disabling Carapace removes the generated Carapace source line and leaves Nushell launch working.
- Check proves disabling Zoxide removes the generated Zoxide source line and leaves Nushell launch working.
- Check proves disabling Starship has a clearly defined prompt result.
- Existing `yzx-nu` checks still prove enabled tools work when defaults are used.
- `nix flake check` if runtime generation changes.
- `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs` if packaged runtime changes.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if this turns into generic shell support.
- Stop if toggles require importing ambient user shell config.
- Stop if the first requested setting is only theoretical and adds config surface without a current user workflow.
notes: Prime-beads review 2026-06-27: defer until there is a concrete user need to disable or inspect managed shell integrations. The bead remains the prerequisite for managed multi-shell selection, but should not appear as ready feature work while the toggle need is theoretical.

title: Add an Alt Shift S manual screen popup
description:
## Why

`yzx screen [style]` already renders Yazelix screens, but invoking it inside a workspace requires opening or reusing a pane and optionally fullscreening that pane. A single deliberate action is useful for three related outcomes: a manual screensaver, casually covering workspace content before stepping away or sharing the terminal, and showing the animated visuals for their own sake. This does not need idle detection or another renderer.

## Evidence

- `runtime/yzx/cli.rs:133-139` delegates `yzx screen` to the packaged `yazelix-screen` command.
- `CHANGELOG.md:285-290` records the fixed style set and existing `random` behavior, which skips the static and logo cards.
- `defaults/zellij/config.kdl:17-46` already defines managed popups with 100% width and height.
- `crates/yzx-config/src/custom_popups.rs:16-39` and the generic popup contract already support argv-based commands and full-size transient panes.
- `defaults/zellij/config.kdl:129` uses `Alt Shift F` for focused-pane fullscreen; no packaged source currently binds `Alt Shift S`.
- Maintainer decision on 2026-07-17: use a full-size popup running the existing random screen, with `Alt Shift S` as the default when available.

## User-visible contract

From any ordinary pane in the active Yazelix tab, `Alt Shift S` opens one transient full-size managed popup running `yzx screen random`. The screen uses `yazelix-screen`'s existing random selection on every new invocation. Normal screen input exits it and restores the prior workspace without changing the underlying pane layout, cwd, editor, sidebar, agent, or tab state.

The action serves as a manual screensaver, casual content cover, and visual interlude. It is not an idle screensaver, session lock, privacy boundary, or protection against another attached client. Direct `yzx screen [style]` and command-menu access remain available for explicit styles.

## Ownership

- `yazelix-screen` remains the sole renderer, style inventory, random selector, input/exit owner, and package owner.
- Yazelix owns the packaged popup declaration, `Alt Shift S` binding, key-reference metadata, and runtime integration.
- The generic popup child retains generic argv, geometry, transient lifecycle, and focus restoration. Change it only if a reproducible generic gap remains after exercising the existing contract.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add one built-in screen popup using the packaged `yzx screen random` command, ordered arguments, 100% width, and 100% height.
- Keep the popup transient/close-on-exit so each invocation starts a new random screen and does not retain hidden state.
- Bind the popup to fixed packaged chord `Alt Shift S`, which is currently free.
- Include the new fixed chord in key-reference and collision validation so user-remapped managed bindings cannot silently collide with it.
- Preserve normal popup displacement, focus restoration, unavailable-command diagnostics, and multi-tab isolation.
- Update README, tutor or key reference where appropriate, CHANGELOG, and README LOC scorecard.

Out of scope:
- Idle timers, automatic activation, lock/authentication, a security claim, background services, or input monitoring outside the running screen.
- A new screen style, configurable random pool, style setting, screen-popup key setting, or second renderer.
- Replacing `Alt Shift F`, changing existing popup keys, or making every `yzx screen` invocation a popup.
- Keeping the screen process alive after dismissal or synchronizing it across sessions/clients.

## Implementation Notes

1. Reuse the built-in popup declaration and toggle message pattern. Give the screen popup a unique stable identity and normal close policy; do not model it as a persistent hidden popup.
2. Invoke the packaged command through store-backed argv, not a shell string or ambient `PATH` lookup.
3. Route `Alt Shift S` through the existing popup plugin and add it to the canonical fixed-key inventory used by config validation and the Ratconfig key reference.
4. Verify that normal input exits through `yazelix-screen`, terminal mode is restored, the popup pane disappears, and focus returns to the previously focused pane.
5. If the generic 100% popup cannot cover enough workspace content to serve as a useful manual cover, report the exact Zellij chrome/geometry limitation before adding layout or pane-frame machinery.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzx-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 and prove popup argv, identity, key-reference, and collision behavior.
- Run `cargo test --manifest-path /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij-popup/Cargo.toml --lib` only if the generic popup child changes.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` should exit 0.
- `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix --profile <tmp>` should install successfully.
- Manual dogfooding should invoke `Alt Shift S` from the editor, sidebar, shell, and an unfocused-agent state; observe different random styles across launches; dismiss with ordinary input; and confirm the original focus, pane layout, tab, and processes are unchanged.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if `Alt Shift S` conflicts with a packaged binding at implementation time; return with the conflict instead of silently selecting another key.
- Stop if the popup requires another renderer, an idle watcher, a keep-alive process, or layout-wide state restoration.
- Stop before changing `yazelix-screen` unless its existing random or exit contract is demonstrably insufficient.
- Stop before changing the generic popup child unless its existing argv, geometry, close, or focus contract fails a focused reproduction.
- Stop calling the feature a privacy or security boundary if underlying content remains accessible to other attached clients or ordinary session controls.

## Maintenance Notes

Effort: S. Risk: LOW/MED because the renderer and popup primitives already exist; final value depends on runtime geometry and focus dogfooding. Confidence: HIGH.

Dependency decision: reuse existing packages and Rust/std paths. Add no crate, package, renderer, daemon, or setting.

Rejected alternatives: a new screen implementation, an idle screensaver, reusing `Alt Shift F`, a persistent hidden popup, a configurable first-slice key/style matrix, and claiming that a visual cover locks the session.
acceptance_criteria:
Contract:

- `Alt Shift S` is the documented fixed packaged action and remains collision-checked against user-remapped managed keys.
- The action opens exactly one transient 100%-width/100%-height popup running packaged `yzx screen random` argv, with no shell or ambient command lookup.
- Every new invocation uses the existing random selector; ordinary input exits, removes the popup, and restores the previous focus and workspace state.
- Direct `yzx screen [style]`, menu access, existing popup keys, and `Alt Shift F` remain unchanged.
- Failure to launch is visible and leaves no stale pane. No idle automation, keep-alive state, new renderer, new setting, or security claim is added.

title: Make Alt z a tab-level zoxide workspace picker
description:
## Why

`Alt z` currently lives inside the managed Yazi keymap, so the user must focus the sidebar before invoking a workspace jump. Workspace selection is a tab-level intent and should be available from the editor, shell, agent-hidden state, or collapsed sidebar without requiring Yazi focus.

## Chosen direction

Make `Alt z` a tab-level Yazelix command backed by the canonical explicit-retarget contract from `yazelix-5vl-f69`. A transient Yazi popup is the preferred picker candidate because it can preserve Yazelix's navigation language, but the implementation should confirm that it is the smallest reliable surface rather than committing to a second persistent Yazi instance or duplicating sidebar state.

## Scope

- Bind one discoverable global `Alt z` action at the Zellij/tab level.
- Present zoxide directory choices without requiring the managed sidebar to be visible or focused.
- Prefer a transient Yazi picker if it can be launched, cancelled, and dismissed cleanly with existing popup/orchestrator ownership; record evidence and choose a simpler transient surface if Yazi cannot satisfy that contract without extra state.
- On selection, invoke the single explicit workspace-retarget primitive. Do not rediscover Git roots or open files through a second bridge protocol.
- Keep cancel harmless and preserve the current tab workspace, editor, sidebar, and agent.
- Decide and document whether the selected directory also opens/focuses the managed editor. The separate editor-free-retarget decision bead owns any additional command/option; do not silently broaden this key into multiple modes.
- Keep popup/sidebar collision behavior compatible with the existing popup and sidebar coordination beads.

## Guardrails

- Do not require sidebar focus, synthesize Yazi keystrokes into the tiled sidebar, or make the sidebar's current cwd the workspace owner.
- Do not introduce another workspace map, Git-root resolver, or tab-name-derived identity.
- Do not add a setting merely to postpone choosing the default interaction.
- Do not fold general file picking, global search, or arbitrary popup framework work into this slice.
- Reuse the main-repo retarget coordinator and existing Helix bridge actions. Do not put picker, workspace, or retarget policy into the Helix fork; child changes are limited to a proven generic popup/orchestrator capability gap.

## Verification

- `Alt z` can be invoked from managed editor, ordinary shell, focused sidebar, and sidebar-hidden states.
- One picker appears in the active tab; cancel leaves every workspace-bound process and root unchanged.
- Selecting a directory calls the canonical retarget path exactly once and produces the same result from every focus state.
- Nested Git, non-Git, and paths containing spaces follow the foundation bead's root policy.
- Existing sidebar navigation and ordinary Yazi opens remain independent of the new global action.
- Key-reference parity, popup lifecycle checks, relevant child checks, flake checks, and a fresh-session dogfood pass succeed.

## Maintenance notes

P2 feature. Preferred UX is a Yazi popup, but implementation choice remains evidence-driven. This bead is blocked by the canonical workspace contract; it does not own that contract.
acceptance_criteria: A single documented tab-level Alt-z action works regardless of focused pane or sidebar visibility. It presents a cancellable zoxide directory picker, preferably through a transient Yazi surface when that remains the smallest reliable implementation. Selection uses the canonical explicit-retarget primitive from yazelix-5vl-f69 without duplicate Git/root/editor protocols. The bead records and implements one default relationship to editor focus/opening; editor-free alternative commands remain separate decision scope.

title: Reassess Yazi materializer extraction with a second consumer
description:
## Why

The bounded Yazi materializer may eventually serve main Yazelix or another runtime, but extracting it before a second consumer would add release, pinning, API, and coordination overhead without deleting any real owner. Reassess only after the local crate's contract is stable and another consumer is ready to adopt it.

## Evidence

- `ARCHITECTURE.md:123-142`: separate repositories require independent users, release cadence, a stable artifact/API, low product coupling, and deletion of a real local owner.
- The local materializer implementation bead establishes a product-coupled config-home contract first.
- Main Yazelix currently owns a broader independent Yazi materialization surface; theoretical reuse is not adoption.

## Decision Criteria

Extract only if all are true:

- A concrete second consumer will call the same input/output contract.
- The second consumer deletes or relinquishes its existing materialization owner.
- The CLI/library contract is stable enough to version independently.
- Runtime-specific paths and policy can be provided without compatibility adapters or duplicate owners.
- Cross-repo release/pin updates are cheaper than atomic local changes.

Keep local if any are true:

- Yazelix Nova remains the only caller.
- Main Yazelix still requires broader import/migration behavior.
- Extraction needs Nova-specific path shims.
- Changes still need to land atomically with `yzx-yazi`.

## Scope

In scope when reactivated:
- Inspect all current callers and the local crate API/CLI.
- Identify the concrete second consumer and the owner it will delete.
- Compare local-crate versus shared-repo release costs.
- Decide keep local or extract.
- If extracting, create implementation beads with exact repository, artifact, API, and migration boundaries.
- Update ARCHITECTURE with the decision.

Out of scope:
- Do not extract code during this decision bead.
- Do not create a generic Yazi configuration framework.
- Do not add compatibility adapters for hypothetical consumers.
- Do not make extraction block power-user functionality.

## Verification

- `rg -n "yzx-yazi-config|yzx-yazi" .` and equivalent searches in the proposed consumer should identify every real caller.
- If no second consumer exists, close with a keep-local decision and no code changes.
- If extraction is selected, the follow-up plan must name the deleted owner and independent verification commands.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop immediately if no concrete second consumer exists.
- Stop if the proposed consumer cannot delete its current owner.
- Stop if the API remains coupled to Yazelix Nova paths or atomic runtime changes.

## Maintenance Notes

Effort: S decision. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.

Current recommendation: keep the crate local. Rejected: extraction based only on possible future reuse.
notes: The bounded power-user Yazi outcome is complete. This remains deferred as an independent architecture decision and should reactivate only when a concrete second consumer can delete an existing owner.
